title: SetHours XFCN 1.2.sit
description: ----------------- SetHours XFCN takes a time string and converts it to 12-hour or 24-hour format, depending on what you supply as parameter 2. If you supply 'AM' or 'PM', the XFCN returns the time in 24-hour format (e.g. SetHours('11:10','PM') returns '23:10'). If you supply 'Set', the XFCN converts a 24-hour time string to its proper 12-hour string. Version 1.2 adds an important change to the result. It now returns the result on one line instead of 2, which makes for easier scripting in most situations. You can parse for the second word of the result (e.g. '11:10 PM') instead of the second line. This version is also much smaller and faster than the previous version. Non-commercial use of this XFCN is free. =George=

title: BasicDB XCMDs 1.1
description: This is a fully functioning demo of version 1.1 of my database external set called BasicDB. It is a demo because write access to database files will expire 14 days after it was created. Notice I said, write access. If you wish to purchase this product, please read the information on the first card. BasicDB is basic. A BasicDB file can contain any number of records. Each record, however, has a fixed structure of the following: 1. A Numeric key (32 bit long integer) 2. A String key of up to 63 characters 3. Textual data limited only by diskspace. (You must also have enough RAM to work with that data [read/write]) 4. Record ID that is unique to that item and is totally controlled by the system. Changes from version 1.0 to 1.1: - Expanded the string key for each database item from 31 characters to 63 characters. This change will make BasicDB 1.0 files incompatible with 1.1 files. You must reload your data through some sort of scripting. I'm sorry about this change, but it was necessary for my project. I'm also sorry that there is no conversion external. - Modified the return value of BasicDBNewItem to return the same full line of data that is returned by the GetIndex call. This will help in maintaining external indexes. - Added user stored long integer and Str127 to the header block. Also created appropriate externals to get/set these values. - Added field to the header block that is the last time that the database was compacted. - Changed all the FlushVol calls to PBFlushFile. Should improve performance a bit. - Created a BasicDBVerify external to check the integrity of the database. - Changed the error string handling to look for the STR# resource by name instead of ID. BasicDB does not have any indexing or anything fancy. It does have searching. You can search for an equality on the numeric key or the string key or a 'contains' search on the text data. Here is a list of the calls. BasicDBCreate BasicDBNewItem BasicDBSetItem BasicDBGetItem BasicDBFindItem BasicDBCompact BasicDBNumItems BasicDBGetIndex I developed BasicDB becuase I wanted to store the data I was working with outside of HyperCard, but I didn't need any sort of fancy stuff and I wanted to store lots of data inside a single record. Brian Blood Maverick Enterprises

title: Broker's Bailiwick 1.04Demo
description: We at QuintesSoft Custom Programming (Quintessential Software) want to invite you to discover our latest service to professional money managers anxious to offer maximum professional services with their Macs. Ignore the older Broker's Bailiwick demos in the business folder of MHC or on MBS) In simple terms, Broker's Bailiwick allows efficient day-to-day navigation through client portfolios and quick performance appraisal by seven investment sub-categories: stocks, bonds, funds, direct investments, cd's, options, and any other one category of your choice. Liberal on-screen support is provided with pop-up help fields to clarify the function and use of most buttons and fields. Among its many features, Broker's Bailiwick computes a client's true annualized rate of return, given both starting and ending dates and amounts along with the entry of all deposit and withdrawal dates and amounts. It summarizes multiple trades for each investment by dividing the sum of realized gains (or loss) by the remaining units of that investment. It concurrently requests current price(s), then computes net unrealized gain (with or without commissions) for any one investment, or any category of investments. As a user, you may merge personalized letters with addresses and salutations for mailings to selected clients, generate mailing labels, do full-feature prospect management, auto-dialing, and move prospects to active account status with assignment of an account number and a mouse click. You are also able 'sift' and sort accounts with easily-revised account marking or sorting routines (based on investment interests, funds coming due, name, city, zip, any key word or phrase). Any account or sub-account is easily converted into another sub-accounts with 4 mouse-clicks and the entry of the new sub-account number, with no re-entry of client data. Whether dealing with a prospect or current client, Each account involves over 30 buttons and 100 fields which include 32 expandable client-data fields, most of which are selectively exposed at the user's discretion. Any account can be effortlessly 'profiled' to expose only relevant fields of interest, such as funds coming due, comments, last call, last mailing, objectives, stocks to monitor, life style, topics of interest, contacts, and alternate phone numbers. Broker's Bailiwick was created under contract with the highest producing broker in his city, working for a major brokerage firm (PaineWebber). He personally solicited our company, QuintesSoft, to create something for the Mac that he was unable to find -- a program that fulfilled his concepts of astute service to his clients. It seemed such a good idea that we stipulated we would retain ownership and put much more effort into program architecture and design from the start, in anticipation of eventual broad commercial release to broker's, money managers, institutional investors, and serious private investors managing multiple accounts. Very competitive bundling is available with Apple or Outbound products, including Powerbooks and Notebooks. Additional customization and trades data file downloading is also available from QuintesSoft Custom Programming 509-779-4488, makers of QUINTESsential SOFTware for the Macintosh.

title: Deed Plotting Real Estate Land
description: ----------------- Deed Plotter 1.1 is an update of Deed Plotter 1.0. It correctly deletes invisible carriage returns from fields when the bearings are deleted. Version 1.0 did not delete carriage returns and loaded the fields up with the invisible stuff. Deed Plotter will map (to a scale that will always fit on a standard 9' Macintosh screen) any set of straight traverse lines that are entered. The resulting figure can be plotted to any desired scale, although any lines that do not appear on the screen are lost. Deed Plotter can be used to add or change traverse lines to force the traverse to close 'balancing the traverse.' The first method of closing adds an additional line which spans the gap between the beginning of the first traverse line and the end of the last traverse line. This added line represents the distance and the angle of the total error in closing the traverse. The second method of closing the polygon is by changing one of the existing lines. This method may be appropriate when existing landmarks confirm that a single gross error may have been made in the original survey description. The final method of closing the traverse distributes the total error among all the lines. The method used is known as the compass rule. This method is more appropriate when random errors have been introduced and the total error is within the predicted accuracy of the surveying techniques used. Any time the traverse is closed by one of these three methods, the area is calculated and displayed in acres.

title: Hacking Physics
description: 4 Sep 1995 POB 5684 (Charleston Marina) Charleston, Or,97420 e-mail freewillee@aol.com or freewill@harborside.com The explicit thesis is that the theory of special relativity is difficult to understand because it is incorrect. The reason it is incorrect is that light (EM) propagates as an energy wave through a medium of aether particles rather than as a ray of photon particles from a source to detection and destruction in a human eye. My difference with special relativity might be semantic but I think it is more profound (if force acts along a straight path through curved space-time or along a curved path through linear space). My interpretation of the inverse square law is that it involves mutual interaction between the two charges ( F = k q1/r q2/r ). At any point in space between the charges, the force from each charge is on a sphere centered where each was when the sphere was generated. The point on the path for the force energy between the charges is where the spheres are tangent. At this instant, the path through space, for all the tangent sphere intersections that were or that will be, is not a straight line through space. Whether space-time is curved or the energy path is curved or whether the mass is increased or the increased path length results in a force that is less effective than expected might just be semantics but should be considered for discussion (i.e., if F=ma or a=F/m then an increase in m or a decrease in F will result in a reduction in a). Perhaps the supporters and the opponents of special relativity are saying the same thing using words having different meanings. For the deflection of a wave from a moving inclined mirror, the reflection is not instantaneous. The plane of the wavefront intersects the plane of the mirror in a line. As the wave progresses, the mirror is moving and the line of intersection moves along the mirror surface. The path of the beam makes a grazing angle with the path through space made by the intersection line of wavefront and mirror (grazing angle is the complement of the incident angle and I used it because the math and the drawing were easier). The paths for a ray and for a point on a wavefront differ after reflection from a moving inclined plane; therefore, the wave-particle duality principle of deBroglie is not correct (i.e., the deflection angle for a wave and for a particle differ when reflected from a moving oblique plane and there can be only one unique path) for this special case. This is the special case for the interferometer used to derive the foundation of special relativity. The result is that there is no phase shift to be predicted in hindsight for the interference pattern.

Beverage Bonanza is an economic simulation game designed to help students understand some basic concepts about economics through a simulated 'real-world', hands-on learning experience. The student gets to be the owner of a lemonade and hot chocolate stand. In order to run this business they will need to make a number of business decisions such as: how many materials to purchase, how much product to make, and what price to sell the product at. The choices the student makes will affect how well she/he does in the game. This program was written by a 7th and 8th grade social studies teacher and his artistic (art teacher) wife. The program has been tested in my classroom, and I found it to be an excellent way to make complex economic concepts come alive for my students. Not only did the students learn a good deal from running the simulation, but they also enjoyed it. (It never hurts to have students actually enjoy something you do in class!) Beverage Bonanza is shareware, IT IS NOT FREE ! If you decide to use the program, you should register it and pay the shareware fee. The cost of registering Beverage Bonanza is $5.00 for a single copy, or $20.00 for a site license. title: Foggy
description: About fifteen years ago a former student sent me [John M. Lawler] a program called foggy that had been circulating underground at IBM which artfully made fun of pompous administrators and their jargon. It was in an internal IBM language, and I translated it into BASIC and Pascal. It was a cute program, but essentially very simple. Somewhat later, I chanced on the Folklore Paper Construction Kit as quoted in Dwight Bolinger's prize-winning Language: The Loaded Weapon, and added its vocabulary of phrases as another option. Finally, I met Anthony Aristar and he shared with me a similar program ( in Lisp) he'd written with phrases collected from the syntactic works of Noam Chomsky. That was the origin of The Chomskybot. It was easy to add these phrases, too. Anthony says he was given these phrases in another program, and didn't know who actually collected them. However, since the Chomskybot has become semi-famous, we have been able to identify the original author as John F. Sowa, who admits putting the phrases together from several of Chomsky's books, including Syntactic Structures, Aspects, and Government and Binding at IBM in the 1980's. Since the real ingenuity in the Chomskybot is the way the phrases fit together, both Anthony and I are delighted to acknowledge Mr. Sowa as The Onlie Begetter of the Chomskybot. (Though we do notice that he hasn't owned up to it on his Web site yet :-) Eventually I got around to making a Macintosh version using HyperCard, to supplement the DOS Version with source code in Pascal. The above description is taken verbatim from http://www-personal.umich.edu/~jlawler/foggy.faq.html . The stack was taken from http://www-personal.umich.edu/~jlawler/ .

title: Tricks of the HyperTalk Masters: Polybuttons
description: Because buttons in Hpyercard are rectangular, it can be hard to use them to cover complex graphic objects with precision. When an object is irregular, slanted, elliptical, or a complex polygon, the only way to simulate a single button over it is to create a series of rectangular buttons and lay them down on the surface like tiles. You must also put the same handler in each button so that pressing each button does what you want. "Polybuttons" allows you to create buttons with multiple sides (polygons) using a special button-maker drawing tool that works like the polygon tool in Hypercard's paint layer. This stack contains the HyperTalk script for "Polybuttons" in its stack script. The script allows for the creation of free-form buttons by creating transparent rectangular buttons around an irregular shape. The useful part of the script is that it will check for overlapping buttons, thus allowing many buttons of various shapes to be in close proximity without danger of their messages being confused.

title: ppat Library

The stack contains over 50 ppats and has buttons to install, delete and load other ppats into itself and other stacks and applications. the tester button allows you to apply the ppat using ADDCOLOR and gives a little documentation on using ppats with ADDCOLOR. This was designed on a Mac Plus with HyperCard 1.01 running under System 6.0.8. I can't guarantee it works under System 7, but would greatly appreciate any bug reports. The stack was scanned with DisInfect and is was uploaded virus-free.

title: Payroll Master 1.5
description: A payroll calculator for the mac. This calculates the amount of an employee's pay cheque, and keeps a record of your employees' payroll history. From the author of Invoice Master, and Inventory Master. Part of the Business Master collection. Shareware, $20.00

title: +Prof. Duck's W. Europe Quiz
description: Staff Pick -- very nicely done Professor Duck's Map Quiz Stacks are Hypercard stacks designed to quiz students on the countries and capitals of a region of the world. All of the regions of the world are covered in 16 different stacks. This upload includes the West Europe stack. The program was developed by a middle school geography teacher who is tired of mundane grading. Having the computer do knowledge level quizzes allows the teacher to use his/her time to teach. It also gives students immediate feedback on how they did, including a report on what they got right and wrong that can be printed out! The stack included in this download is an entirely functional sample of what these stacks are all about. If you would like information on how to get more of these stacks for a reasonable shareware fee contact me at: veronaemh (on America Online).

title: Bicycle Gear Calculator 4.0
description: Bicycle Gear Calculator 4.0 Tuesday, June 23, 1998 This stack lets cyclists examine existing gearing configurations and helps in designing new ones for multi-speed bicycles (up to 21 gears).

title: Compuserveindex2
description: A catalog of all of the 700 forums on CompuServe, with discriptions of each forum's purpose and other relevent info.

title: Keep Track of Businesses EASY!
description: ------From the people at PeterSoft----- Are you having trouble keeping track of all those Business cards? Are you flummoxed by those little pieces of paper and how to organize them? Then Try 'BcardDB' it works great! --------

title: King John
description: An annotated arrangement of the play King John, by William Shakespeare. This stack was authored by Mark Zimmermann, and is part of the Shakespeare Stack Project This stack has been released as Free Software under the GNU General Public License.

title: Mac Product Database in HC
description: This is a stack I've been working on for a while... It's a relatively small (173 entries) database of a wide variety of Mac products. While it's certainly not a complete listing, it covers many product types and has details on some hard drives, monitors, scanners, printers, and more. If you're thinking of buying a new piece of hardware or software for your Mac, it might do you some good to look for it in here. Even if the one you're thinking of isn't included, several similar ones may be. (and probably are) It also gives some mail-order company names and numbers for when you do decide to buy that product. IMBO, I think this is a very helpful thing to have. :) It's been stuffed with StuffIt 2.0, and scanned with Disinfectant 3.2.

title: RTF XCMDs with source (v1.2)
description: Some shareware XCMDs to read and write styled text in Rich Text Format (RTF). Can be used to conveniently manipulate styled text in HyperCard, or to exchange styled text with Word. Can handle more than 30Kbytes of text. Includes Think C source.

title: WeatherBOOK DEMO
description: This is a 2-card DEMO of a huge meteorological database containing detailed weather information for over 300 US cities. The complete World WeatherBOOK has data for over 600 cities worldwide. The front-end interface is not included in this DEMO. The user can customize and add cities to the full version. Also included is real-time graphing of temperature data. I welcome your feedback.

title: DXMap 1.1 - Ham/Shortwave
description: This Hypercard stack has many feature of a commerical product called DX Helper. Grayline Map, sun position, Time Zones, Country Name and Amateur Radio Call prefix list. Provides longitude, latitude, antenna bearing and distance to any point clicked on map or country selected (gives Ham call prefix for country) on a scrolling list from your location. It's a well done stack worth having for any Ham or SWLer.

description: ----------------- Sound from Pink Floyd's Dark Side of the Moon album. 'Home, home again.' Use it to spruce up your home buttons, or to welcome you to your home stack...

title: Two Gentlemen of Verona
description: An annotated arrangement of the play Two Gentlemen of Verona, by William Shakespeare. This stack was authored by Mark Zimmermann, and is part of the Shakespeare Stack Project This stack has been released as Free Software under the GNU General Public License.

title: Personal Directory 2.0.1
description: Personal Directory 2.0.1 is a HyperCard stack that provides you with a flexible way to access, organize, and print your personal database of names, addresses, phone numbers, and URLs. It automatically sorts single and multiple name entries, tracks birthdays and anniversaries, and supports drag and drop of information between fields. Use this stack to print mailing labels and desk-size organizer sheets. You can also categorize your entries using one or more categories, copy or print entries based on categories or events, and enter free text notes. Navigation through the stack is easy, too: simply click on letter buttons to access entries via pop-up lists, and use the left and right arrow keys or buttons to move ahead or back one card. If you've got scanned pictures of family and friends, you can use this stack as an online photo album. In addition, Personal Directory automatically formats phone numbers and can autodial them for you. Feel free to try out the demo version of this stack. The demo version is fully functional, but limits you to a maximum of 10 cards. The 10 card restriction can be lifted by registering your Personal Directory and paying the $15 shareware fee. If you have questions or comments, please e-mail me at TomRydberg@aol.com. Thanks for trying it out! ------------------ Scanned for viruses with Disinfectant 3.7.1 and merryXmasVacine.

title: Dreamtime: A Hypertext

Downloading is allowed provided that you do not charge anyone else for this text, and that you not remove the author's name and copyright notice. "Dreamtime" Â© 1992 by Stuart Moulthrop.

title: Book Keeper 2.0
description: Book Keeper is a stack designed to manage your personal library. It was designed using WindowScript with additional externals created with CompileIt!. Book Keeper comes with a few of my favorite authors entered in to show the basic functioning of the program. Book Keeper keeps information on your books indexed by author and allows you to search for books and authors, print lists and designate categories. Book Keeper is shareware. A fee of $5.00 is asked if you use this stack.

title: CreateFolder XCMD 1.3

title: HyperCard Movie Stacks
description: HyperCard Movie Stacks Wednesday, June 24, 1998 A complete set of stacks for movie editing and creation.

title: Alert Manager 1.1.SIT
description: To date several programmers have written XCMDs and XFCNs that extend HyperCard by providing tools for using custom modal dialogs. Most notable of these is Jay Hodgdon's ShowDialog XCMD because it has the most features, and it is the most flexible. None, however, specifically address the use of alerts, or if they do, they are limited in some fundamental way. Consequently, I have developed, and implemented in this stack, the Alert Manager, a complete system for displaying alert dialogs using ShowDialog XCMD. Simply put, the Alert Manager allows you to call any standard-type alert dialog from a script with a minimal amount of HyperTalk code. This is accomplished through a set of handlers that control the size, appearance, and type of alert to display. Furthermore, all the necessary DLOG/DITL (dialog) templates are included to save you the trouble of hacking around in ResEdit. With the Alert Manager, all this is quite easy to implement, and even easier to handle in scripts. No conditional statements are required in your scripts to branch to the proper alert action. This is all handled by the Alert Manager scripts.

title: AnimIcon 1.0.1
description: AnimIcon is a color icon plotting utility for HyperCard, and is the only one that will not produce a flash if you decide to move the icon. It does this by doing all of its drawing offscreen. =============== Scanned with Disinfectant 3.6

title: BarbieCollecting.sit
description: ----------------- This is a set of five stacks I developed to keep track of my vintage Barbie doll collection. They're designed to handle what I specifically needed to organize (Barbie dolls, Barbie clothes, Skipper dolls, Skipper clothes, and special items) but can be easily modified to work with just about any sort of doll collection. Have fun...

title: Baseball Addresses

This is small-fee shareware (only $5, or less), but it's worth it! Enjoy! Send suggestions and comments to me, MyteeDuck.

title: Card Organizer 1.0
description: Card Organizer 1.0 is a shareware 2.2X stack, ©1996 Ken Dunham, that enables users to easily organize the order of cards within a HyperCard stack. You can also rename cards, delete cards, or merge cards with another stack.

title: Cours à cent

title: Creepin' Critter Clocks 1.1.1
description: Welcome to Creepin' Critter Clocks, the fun way for kids to practice their time-telling skills! Creepin' Critter Clocks is an arcade-style learning game that is both challenging and fun. Kids swat bugs (the "Creepin' Critters") or pop bubbles that correspond to the correct answer to a time-telling problem. Points are earned, in true arcade game fashion, for correct answers. The amount of time available to answer a problem can be adjusted to suit the skill level of the child. The arcade-style scoring provides an incentive for decreasing the amount of time, since lower time settings increase the possible scores.

title: Customer Tracking Package(Rolode
description: ----------------- This is a more involved address book-type application, aimed at the business user. Created for CPSC-606, Software Engineering, at Texas A M. Includes a brief user's manual for explanations. Best feature is that the program helps you keep track of what's due, and what's past due. See the manual for details Thanks to Jim, Dean, Mike, Steve, Khanh, and Diane for their help! TC

description: A Hypercard EarthStak

description: ----------------- Childish Bathroom noises....but a nice bloom county graphic of opus.

description: ----------------- Converts a given string to a seven or eleven digit phone number, stripping out all non-numeric characters.

title: Fractions 2.0
description: XFCN to compute fractions and prime numbers.

description: HyperCard stack that includes an XCMD that translates HGPL to PICT format. Source code included.

title: Hollow Text Stack
description: This stack contains a font called 'Chicago Hollow' which is the font of disabled buttons and menu items in the standard Macintosh interface (disabled = greyed out characters). You can install it in any stack with the Font/DA Mover. Chicago Hollow may be used to make HyperCard buttons act more like standard Macintosh buttons; see the scripts of the buttons on this card (and the script of the card itself) for examples.

description: ----------------- HPGL to PICT conversions are done with this stack which contains the hp2pict XFCN.

title: HyperDimensions 1.0
description: Placing this stack in the Stacks-In-Use makes the functions available to your own stacks. HyperDimensions 1.0 can be used as a standalone converter/calculator, the stack is organized in two backgrounds; 'Converter' which demonstrates the functions' precision, fractional formats and calculates the conversion of dimensions, allowing conversions to be saved one per card. 'Calculator' which provides the ability to enter values as numbers/dimensions and have HC perform the following math for you; SUM ... add a column of dimensions DIVIDE ... divide a dimension by a number or dimension MULTIPLY ... multiply a dimension by a number or dimension 'Clipboard' buttons for each math operation transfer the entered values as equations and results to the clipboard for easy pasting into other application documents. (Requires HC 2.3 or MHC Player [available in MHC AOL library]). This stack is StrokeWare for personal Use, copyrighted for any other use. Hope this 'Measures Up' for those of you who need to work with dimensions in HC. ENJOY! JEFF CROSSLEY

title: HyperGobbledygook 1.0a
description: gobbledygook n [irreg. fr. gobble ]: wordy and generally unintelligible jargon HyperGobbledygook creates random samples of the kind of bureaucratic, academic, and military doubletalk that makes life in the English-speaking world so nervewracking for people who speak English. The JARGON button in the corner of the main window will present and save individual items of gobbledygook, one at a time. Use the Jargon menu to create, save, and print mass quantities of the stuff. Think of yourself as a firefighter fighting fire with fire, or a Tibetan monk trying to invoke all ten million names of God. If we generate enough random jargon, maybe it will go away.

title: Illinois HyperTextBook 4

title: Math & Science Equations
description: Very helpful equation 'figure-outer'!! Helpful for math students of any subject. Contains appx. 50 equation editors. Enter parameters and equation results are displayed. Requires 14' monitor. FREEWARE

title: Penguin Utilities.SIT
description: ----------------- This stack allows you to perform 15 functions on multiple objects. These functions include align, delete, edit script, export text, export script, hide, get info, change names, renumber (useful!), select, show, change styles, change text attributes (really useful!), move to background, move to card, and unlock text. All you do is select any number of objects from a list, then select the function from another list, and the stack performs that function on everything youve selected. It is especially useful when you have a lot of similar objects (fields or buttons) which change during developement of a stack.

title: Powers 1.0
description: A XFCN to simplify exponents.

title: VCR Tapes Catalog
description: A program for cataloging your VCR tapes collection. Could also be adapted for CD's & records. In addtion to detail records, the program maintains directories sorted by Tape Number and by selection title. Has print capablities too.

title: XCMD Register
description: About the XCMD Register At the Spring '88 Developers Conference in San Jose, I spent many hours locked up in my room creating a stack for Apple to use at the conference. I needed to do a certain task that was exceptionally slow in HyperTalk. I thought, 'somebody probably has written an XCMD to do this, but how do I find it?' During one of the conference sessions on HyperCard copyrights and trademarks, a guy asked a question about a system to track external commands and functions. Everyone looked at each other as if to say, 'that's such a good idea, somebody must be doing it already!' Well, here it is! After those two events sunk into my brain, I decided to try it. After looking at all the names like HyperWare (which is a trademark now), freeware, shareware, beerware, mailware, betaware, vaporware, and stackware, I decided on a name. This is called 'forehead-ware.' As you use it you are obliged to slap yourself on the forehead (a la a V-8 commercial) and say, 'why didn't anybody think of this before?'

title: Adventure Holiday
description: ----------------- Here's an opportunity for you to take an animated adventure in Super Natural British Columbia, Canada, without leaving your Mac. The 'Outdoor and Adventure Vacation' guide on disk takes you on an entertaining, interactive tour of the sights and sounds of B.C. with an animated tour guide named Eddie. He'll take you salmon fishing, scuba diving, whale watching, and mountain climbing, and even provide a little commentary for your enjoyment. Produced by Motion Works Inc. of Vancouver, an award winning multimedia Macintosh software developer, and the Ministry of Tourism in British Columbia, the 'Outdoor and Adventure Vacation' package is available free of charge by calling 1-800-663-6000. You can get a preview of this stack by downloading this stack.

title: Bible Promise 3.2
description: Replaces 'Bible Promise 3.0' and 'The Bible Promise Stack', both in MHC/History,Religion This is the long awaited version 3.0 of The Bible Promise Stack. There have been many features added and some cosmetic improvements. For those of you not familiar with the Bible Promise Stack, it is similar to the Bible Promise Books found in Christian Book Stores. If you need to see what the Bible says on a certain topic then this is the stack you need. It includes the NIV and King James Version of the Bible. The stack is $15 shareware, but if you are unable to pay for the stack and want to use it anyway, then please read the special shareware notice included with the stack.

title: ++ColoringDemo
description: This file contains two color stacks authored by Tsutom Anayama which demonstrate various uses of color in HyperCard geared to produce a fast display. Here is what Mr. Anayama says about his stack : 'ColoringSet-1 shows you several methods of easy and rapid color pict show including color animation, painting and pict resource making. ColoringSet-2 shows you direct color painting on a card, rapid presentation of pict resource data base and pict resource making.' He has other stacks available on his website at http://potato.edu.toyama-u.ac.jp **************** This stack has been checked for Mac and HyperCard Viruses. Checked with Virex 5.6

title: CompileIt! 2.5 & Double-XX In...
description: Information about Heizer Software's CompileIt! 2.5 release. Includes: * Press Release * CompileIt! 2.5 Release Notes * Example INIT (aka Extension) * TinyEdit Application (Built with Double-XX/Lite) Also discusses Double-XX and Double-XX/Lite which are tools used to create stand-alone applications from XCMDs. Double-XX/Lite is included with CompileIt! 2.5 as a free bonus.

title: DECnet NodeInfo v1.0 XFCN & Appl
description: NodeInfo v1.0 enables you to quickly view the DECnet node name area, number and executive characteristics identifier of your local node on a DECnet/Macintosh network. Included is a sample DECNet data file 'Network Data' which the NodeInfo v1.0 will attempt to interrogate. Place 'Network Data' into your System Folder in your boot drive to test the NodeInfo v1.0 software. The NodeInfo application displays a NoteAlert dialog showing the the DECnet node name, area number, node number, and DECNet Executive Characteristics Identifier. The NodeInfo XFCN returns a 4 item, comma delimited list containing the DECnet node name, area number, node number, and the DECNet Executive Characteristics Identifier. Both the NodeInfo v1.0 application and XCMD are freeware. Any comments would be welcome. Please ask me for permission to use this in commercial applications. Karl K. Nicoletti AOL: NICOLETTI Voice: 303-966-6825 (wk)

title: Geographax: A Test of Your Knowledge of the World of Places 1.1
description: Geographax is a HyperCard stack that presents a few items from 2000 unusual geography questions. It should hold the interest of a wide range of people, from young people (middle school or so) to serious geography buffs.

title: Get DA Names XCMD 1.2.SIT
description: ----------------- An XCMD to get the names of the current DAs available and an XFCN to return the number of DAs. You'll find this stack incredibly useful for one simple reason...you won't have to type the exact, perfectly spelled names of your DA's to launch them.

title: GetManager 1.0

title: HyperCard/QuickTime palette-f...
description: This is the final version of my HyperCard/QuickTime remote control palette (I think all bugs are now gone!). I am sorry if any of you had problems with the previous versions. I think I got it right this time:) The remote control palette has been redone to now resemble 3-D 'buttons' and I have added seven additional ways to view the QuickTime movies. This palette will allow a HyperCard/QuickTime author to test out a movie in a stack under development. This testing will allow size and position changes before the scripts have been written. Coordinates for the movie can then be determined. I think that you will find it to be a big time saver. The movie to be viewed must be named 'Take 1' for the palette to recognize it and should be in the same folder as the stack containing the palette. I have rewritten the instructions on the stack for using and transferring/removing the palette from your own stack under development. It should be clearer now.

description: A Hypercard stack for searching WAIS (Wide Area Information Servers).

This is a pre-release version. All features are enabled, but the maps stack only includes California. Contact screen name 'Wildernet' for more info. FREE.

title: MessageOnHold
description: MessageOnHold is designed to play music and/or messages to telephone callers who you put on hold. You need to have a way to get the music from your Mac's sound port to your phone system. For some systems, you simply make up a cord from an earphone cord and plug it into your phone system (and Mac). Some phone systems require some programing so that they will play the music. Once connected, MessageOnHold plays the sounds in the 'Music' and 'Messages' lists. It normally alternates between the two so that callers hear a message (such as 'Thank you for holding' then a music piece, then a message and so on forever. I haven't tested this stack with a lot of other programs or tried it on different Mac models. I anticipate that it will be used on a dedicated (Semi-Retired) Mac with a minimum of extensions such as the old SE/30 I'm using. I'm running HC 2.3 but I think it will run under 2.2. Staff note: This stack was not tested for functionality with a telephone. This stack has been checked for Mac and HyperCard viruses with virex 5.6

ENJOY WITH BLUE SKIES, WHITE CLOUDS, AND A STRONG WIND!!! - Rome Dome :)

This document is bundled with the PaperLess MicroViewer application, version 2.0.1, written by by Michael Long and Michael Zoran of Nine to Five Software. POB 1899, Boulder, CO, 80308, 303.443.4104.

title: Lemonade

title: :Mac Tips & Tricks 1.0

It is not, however, a compilation of easter eggs - for that I would recommend Apple Easter Eggs (created by Daniel Fanton), a DocMaker app widely available on the Net. Mac Tips & Tricks contains info on a wide range of subjects, from navigating an open file dialog box, troubleshooting the Finder and hacking the system to changing a file's icon.

title: LineSorter Stack
description: ----------------- LineSorter is an easy way to organize long item lists in alphabetical order. It was originally created for speeding up the process of creating and organizing galleys of type for camera-ready art, but it could be used for any list you wish. Type in your list manually, or import them through the clipboard.

title: Love's Labour's Lost
description: An annotated arrangement of the play Love's Labour's Lost, by William Shakespeare. This stack was authored by Mark Zimmermann, and is part of the Shakespeare Stack Project This stack has been released as Free Software under the GNU General Public License.

title: MatchLine XFCN 2.0
description: ----------------- MatchLine 2.0 returns the number of the line in a list matching the pattern string. It also allows you to search within items, in which case it will return both the line number and the matching item number in a 2-item result. Syntax: MatchLine(,[,]) The last parameter is optional, and describes whether to compare the entire line or to compare items. All non-commercial usage is free. Enjoy! =George

title: MathHill 1.0
description: Third-grade level math problems as flash-card style addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division. Fun and easy way to practice math. Choose a math option and type the answers. Climb up MathHill with each correct answer and try to reach the top of the hill. Top five scores are entered into the Top MathHill Climbers honor list. This program has been child tested and approved. Note: your monitor screen must be able to display color or you won't see the graphics. Shareware fee: $10

title: MedLibMgr 4.0
description: This is my medical reference library managing stack. I have spent many hours writing, fixing, adding to, optimizing, and using this stack. I hope you find it as useful as I do.

title: MegaHome II 3.01:New Home Stack
description: MegaHome II 3.01 is a fully-documented, full-featured replacement for the HyperCard 2.x 'Home' stack. Released on January 1, 1992, it reflects exactly thirteen months' hard work on improving the stack since the release of version 1.1 on December 1, 1990. It is offered as $15 shareware from Earth Systems. MegaHome II provides numerous features not found in the Home stack shipped with HyperCard. It features a complete Finder-like interface featuring dragging, selecting and double-clicking. It also includes a moderately secure 'safe' card, known as Ft. Knox, to guard your confidential material. Another useful feature is a built-in screen-saver. All features are documented in the included MegaHome HELP stack. NOTE: Version 3.01 is functionally equivalent to version 3.0. If you are currently using version 3.0, there is NO NEED to download this archive. The only changes made in 3.01 were the addition of custom icons and the updating of the version number to reflect a final release rather than the beta-test version incorrectly indicated in 3.0. MegaHomex II's third release boasts a number of new features not found in versions 1.1 and 2.0.: -The Diagnostics stack has been incorporated into the home stack; -The confusing and virtually useless Prefs stack has been eliminated; -F15 now activates the Screen Saver, and the Screen Saver has been rewritten to resize itself to the size of the current screen, as well as other new features; -'Power Commands' are now standard; -Displays can now be temporarily hidden; -'Atheist Mode' has been added for those who do not celebrate December 25; -Script Checksums have been added to protect some code from hazardous modixcation; -User-dexnable Auto-Compacting Thresholds have been added; -A message is shown in the message box during Auto-Compacting; -A few handlers have been compiled into XCMDs and XFCNs for greater speed; -A new Intercept, compactMe (for programmers), has been added; -A 'Shrink' button has been added for users of small screens and MultiFinder or System 7; -MegaHome's menus are hidden when in other stacks; -Ft. Knox displays the stopwatch cursor properly, and security has been greatly improved; -Level 4 Support for the soon-to-be-released Module Manager has been added; -The Icon Picker is finally here--you no longer need to worry about stupid ID numbers!; -You are no longer prompted upon quitting; the quit command functions normally. 'Installing MegaHome is brief and simple. From the Finder, simply get rid of your old Home stack, and rename MegaHome, 'Home'. The next time you start up HyperCard, MegaHome will be installed.' MegaHome *must* be installed as the current Home stack. If one attempts to open the MegaHome stack when it is not installed as the current home stack, MegaHome will search for global variables that it assumed were installed upon startup time. Errors will result when it discovers that such variables were not initialized as they should have been.

title: Menus for HyperCard
description: Has Hypertalk XCMDS and XFCNs for adding menus to the menu bar. It looks like a pretty straightforward procedure to add them into your own stacks.

title: Multiplication Horror BugFix
description: The tiny stack fixes a small bug in 'The House of Multiplication Horror.' Just put it in the same folder as the 'The House of Multiplication Horror,' open it, and click the AlphaDog button. It takes care of the modifications automatically and even disposes of itself.

title: MusiCalc 4.4
description: MusiCalc is a multi-functional HyperCard-based music and time calculator which will add, subtract, multiply, and divide time on a scale of minutes and seconds. Entries are free-form and may be corrected, annotated, copied, cut, pasted, and recalculated. MusiCalc consists of 4 calculator elements: 1) MusiCalc, a calculator for musical time relationships (calculates time from bars & tempo, bars from time & tempo, and tempo from bars & time, and converts beats-per-minute to frames-per-beat & vice-versa); 2) TimeCalc, a time calculator which properly adds, subtracts, multiplies and divides minutes, seconds, frames, and bits (with optional entry of tenths, hundredths, etc. in place of, or combined with, these subdivisions); 3) BarCalc, a formula evaluator which simplifies computing the length of a piece of music, particularly music with changing meters and/or repeating sections whose measure-number count does not correspond to elapsed beats (BarCalc can be used for any kind of multi-part formula evaluation); and 4) SMPTECalc, which calculates sum and difference times using a stored SMPTE timecode number and MusiCalc or TimecCalc results, facilitating re-synchronization.

title: Patent Copyright Trademark Pr...
description: The stacks were written by Professor Thomas G. Field, Jr., Director of the Innovation Clinic of Franklin Pierce Law Center (Concord NH) based on almost 20 years of experience in answering questions from people described above. Well over 80,000 copies of these stacks have been distributed in booklet form by the Law Center and the U.S. Small Business Administration. Stack descriptions: (1) Information is a general index to the remaining stacks. (2) So You Have an Idea is a basic discussion of the relationship between patents and the market value of inventions. Discusses the need for counsel in making outside submissions, the need for prior art searches, invention promoters and other matters of special interest to independent inventors - particularly from a cost standpoint. (3) Seeking Cost-Effective Patents is a detailed discussion of ways to evaluate intellectual property options in view of the nature of an invention and its market value. Contains specific strategies for controlling ever-increasing patent costs in the face of market uncertainty. (4) Trademarks and Business Goodwill is a basic discussion of strong versus weak marks, the need for searches, the value of state and federal registrations and other matters of importance to owners of small businesses. (5) Avoiding Patent, Trademark and Copyright Problems is written primarily for owners of small businesses. (6) Copyright in Visual Arts and (7) Copyright for Computer Authors address the importance of copyright registration and notice, works for hire, deposit requirements, registration of multiple works, the need for counsel in licensing and other matters of interest to free lance artists, computer authors, craftspeople and small entrepreneurs.

title: Penguin Registration Numbers
description: Penguin Registration Numbers; prg support aid

title: Nevada HyperTextBook 4
description: A HyperText book of cities and places in the state of Nevada. Lists interesting facts and figures.

title: NewFolder XCMD
description: XCMD which allows you to create new folders.

title: PlayMovie 4.0 XCMD (Director ...
description: An external allowing you to play a Director 4.0 movie from within HyperCard. This external is not officially supported by MacroMedia. I was told by MM that an engineer had donated his time to write the command. The limited documentation says to refer to the Player 3.0 stack that shipped with Director 3.0 as the documentation for this stack. But there are major differences between the way this XCMD works now and the way it did in the past. The old version supported the movieShowMenu and movieHideMenu parameters which allowed scripters to control HC's menubar when a movie played. The new version ALWAYS hides the menubar, and it will remain hidden until the command playMovie movieClose completely dumps the player from RAM. This can be VERY distracting if you have designed an 'application' with your own menus!

description: This stack is a flash card and interactive quiz maker. Interactive quiz results are given in a percentage. It is Shareware; however, students and educators are fee-exempted.

title: The 1994 Tomato War

It fixes a few bugs in CompileIt! 2.1 including the following: * 2.1 did not always convert well into a SuperCard 1.6 project. * using the params function on empty params could crash * p-string selector code constants (like those used in Gestalt) did not work correctly. If you have a version of CompileIt! less than 2.0 of CompileIt!, you'll need to call Heizer Software at 800-888-7667 to purchase an upgrade.

title: URL Database 1.1
description: This stack is a simple database for storing URLs. It has a couple of features that make it very usable: 1) There's an Index card that displays a list of the URLs in the stack. The list can be sorted by name or date. This makes it easy to view the contents of the stack and directly access any of the cards; 2) The user can create categories to organize the URLs into manageable groups. The stack makes it easy to copy a URL to the Clipboard to paste into a browser; this is done by simply clicking on a name in the list. The stack can export the URLs to an HTML file, which can then be opened in a browser. Adding a new URL to the stack is easy: copy a URL, from anywhere, and click on the New button. The URL will be automatically pasted into the URL field (for this to work, the URL must contain the string "http").

title: FindFile XFCN
description: Version 1.1 This XFCN mimics the 'Find File' DA from Apple. It can be used to find files whose names match a given string. FindFile requires two parameters. The first is a pathname to a folder where the search should begin. FindFile will also look in all folders in that folder, and all folders in those folders, etc.

description: For HC 1.2 , this stack takes away the burden of being a DungeonMaster for Advanced D&D. It does many of the time consuming number crunching.

title: Stand-alone stacks!
description: This stack will explain how you can create a stand-alone HyperCard stack (a stack that doesn't require the HyperCard application) with HyperCard and ResEdit.

title: HyperBrain
description: A multi-user brainstorming tool. For anyone who has a file server and multiple Macs.

title: The Dairy Debate
description: The Dairy Debate Stack considers the implications of the use of recombinant bovine growth hormone (BGH) by the dairy industry, and rotational grazing, for animal health, consumers, the environment, farm economics, and rural communities. Its purpose is to broaden and inform the public debate over dairy technology and food safety.

title: ButtonTalk 1.4.1
description: ButtonTalk™ is an easy to use tool for creating interactive fiction on Macintosh Classic. It may also be useful for documentation, for scripts or prototypes of interactive products and for exploring ideas for traditional fiction.

To leave a room click on the bottom of the screen a door or a window (in pits click on the top). I hope you enjoy Castle Quest. Thank you. title: Apple Events XFCN
description: XFCN, Think C source code and a stack allows you to use AppleEvents in System 7 to do things like OpenApp, CloseApp, OpenDoc and PrintDoc.

title: EmailLog 2.3.1 -- UPDATE
description: Use this stack to save your email correspondence and to compose email offline. Your AOL mailbox only saves messages for a limited time, so you can't just let old messages sit there indefinitely and still have them when you need them. This way you have all your correspondence at your fingertips - and you can use hypercard's find routines to locate text. EmailLog allows you to save email text files, sort and print them, find text, format text (one keystroke removes EOL's and extra spaces), compose messages offline, and open AOL with the message automatically put on the clipboard. Use EmailLog if you subscribe to any mailing lists - use a separate copy for each mailing list or correspondent, and you won't have to search through zillions of little text files to find what you are looking for. Or save all messages in one stack and sort them by correspondent. EmailLog also works for messages on FirstClass BBS's. Features include: compose multiple messages offline, select correspondents from a pop-up button, store often-used email addresses, auto-quote and auto-reply to a message, add a signature with one click. Multiple-card printing and exporting to file are supported. You can (if you choose) automatically mark for printing when importing and automatically log messages after sending; sort chronologically, or by correspondent or subject (message threads are grouped together); and import a whole folder of saved messages at once. As always, if you've been using an earlier version of EmailLog, you can import the present contents of your EmailLog stacks into a copy of this latest version. This is a very useful stack if you're carrying on any significant amount of email correspondence. If you subscribe to any mailing lists, as I do, and want to archive messages, try this out! (I have an archive of over 700 messages from one list which allows me to find and export/print old postings on a given topic within minutes.) Note: An XCMD was inadvertently not attached to version 2.3 of EmailLog. Please use this version -- 2.3.1. My apologies for any problems the defective stack caused. This stack has been checked for Mac and HyperCard viruses with Virex 5.6 Previous DL count: 38

title: Mexico
description: This is a program that I created for my class during a unit on Mexico. It is filled with facts about Mexico and it's people.

title: NetTalk v1.0
description: This is a simple little stack for sending messages between people on a network. It is very similar to the Apple Events Primer, but much simpler to use, and smaller. You can save your conversations to a TEXT file, and read them in a word processor or in NetTalk. Unfortunately, program sharing(and maybe file sharing) need to be on for it to work.

title: Phone Timer/Fee Calculator
description: ----------------- This is a neat little stack for lawyers or anyone who makes telephone calls and bills for those calls on an hourly basis. Extremely easy to use, this stack will instantly compute the length of a phone call and provide you with the fee to charge your client for that call, based on your hourly billing fee.

title: ResetMyColorPLEASE!
description: Ever find that a color program has messed up your standard color pallete? Does the ADDCOLOR XCMD leak out to your windows? Normally, you would restart or go into the monitors control panel to reset the colors. This big button will do all that in a second or two. This stack uses the INCOLOR(FROM HEIZER SOFTWARE), and the ADDCOLOR XCMD(FROM APPLE)

title: Lastexit2
description: Last Exit to Hypercard is an innovative extender for Hypercard that is authored by Sam Deane of Ultralab in England. This package includes ULTRALAB, an XCMD that links Hypercard to a folder inside your System folder. That new folder can contain all XCMDs and XFCNs used with HyperCard.

title: Library Stack 1.2
description: ----------------- Library Stack 1.2 is a disk cataloger. It will read the Volume Name, File Name, File Type, and File Size from each file on a floppy disk into the proper field in the stack. It has two additional fields for entering Key Words and a Description about the file(s). You can search for a file by its File Name, its File Type, or by a Key Word. You can also search for a disk by its name. It is much improved over version 1.1. It will now put files in a folder onto a single card, regardless of the files' names. It files the cards in alphabetical order by File Name. When you search for a file, you can use more than one word. If you do not exactly remember the name of the file you are looking for, it will find what you type within a longer string of words. When you leave the stack, if the free space is > 10K, it tells you the amount of disk space needed to compact the stack, the amount of disk space available, and lets you decide if you want to do it or not. Shareware. $10.

MacNursing 3.0 is a 4th Dimension patient database system for small hospitals, nursing units, and home health care. This stack is a demo of the MacNursing Care Plans Hypercard Stack. It contains several problems and allows the user to print out a patient care plan of the marked problems. The actual stack, with over 100 nursing problems, is available by sending $30.00 to MacNursing, 465 Waller, San Francisco, CA 94117. Information regarding all products and services can be obtained by calling (415) 281-5883.

Whatever you create with Museum can be viewed on any Macintosh. Here are a few standard Museum features... - Rock-Solid, Stand-Alone document! - Supports Pictures, Text with Style, & Sound - Suppports Quicktime-compressed JPEG/PICT - Runs on EVERY Macintosh/Monitor configuration - Many features for you to customize (try it in Color!) - Extremely easy to use, very small, and fun! ------------------------------------------------- What's new in Museum v0.5... - Full-Screen Slideshow - Multi-Level Destruct - Custom Default-Size (for Patrons ) - Scrolling Pictures - New Mailing Address!!! Thanks very much for your Shareware support! Please note the NEW mailing address... Rustle Laidman PO BOX 39A76 Los Angeles, CA 90039 ======================================================== ATTN: Registered Museum Members, here on AOL!!! If you would like to upgrade your Subscriber or Supporter level Museum, send your AOL screen name, (& real name) to RUSTLE2. ========================================================

title: Numerical Routines Stack.sit
description: ----------------- This stack contains many XCMD's and XFCN's to greatly enhance the computing power of hypercard. We use Hypercard as a Data Acquisition and Manipulation tool at work, and were dissapointed in the mathematical sluggishness of it. This stack contains XCMDs and XFCNs to do the following: Columnar math, 3D plotting (and movies), Scientific notation, Linear and Polynomial fits, Chi-square analysis,X-Y scatterplotting,Radix-2 FFT and FFT smoothing, and a general purpose Calculator XFCN which is wonderful. I have worked with the authors from the very begining, and believe that this is the most comprehensive set of mathematical routines available for hypercard. If you have any questions, feel free to send me a comment - I will relay any questions I can't answer to the authors... Take it!

title: Printer Driver Editor
description: This is a quick and dirty stack that allows you to change the Screen Frequency (lines per inch of a screen) and the Screen Angle of an Aldus printer description file (PPD). This will allow you to print a PageMaker document (with ANY type of graphic files) with a screening higher or lower than the default screen of you printer.

title: STEROIDS lite
description: STEROIDS lite is a 50 structure subset of STEROIDS, a HyperCard stack of more than 650 steroid structures, including anabolics, catabolics, cardenolides, bufadienolides (frog toxins), estrogens and other female and male hormones, steroidal vitamins (D-series), anti-inflammatories, and many more. STEROIDS is designed as a structural reference work for organic, medicinal and biochemists, high school and college instructors and students, as well as others with an interest in an important class of biologically active compounds. STEROIDS can be used as a teaching aid, and can be readily modified for use as the core of a broad information database. STEROIDS lite is a sampler of the larger work, demonstrating its organization and diversity. STEROIDS lite is Copyright 1995, William A. Hoffman III. All rights are reserved. STEROIDS lite may be distributed intact freely, but may not be included in any commercial product without express written permission from the author.

title: Inflate XCMD 1.2
description: TYPE:Shareware XCMD SYSTEM 7: Compatible Author's email address: alex@metcalf.demon.co.uk The Inflate XCMD lets you easily add balloon help to all of your HyperCard stacks. Help balloons can be added to buttons, fields, areas of a card, and more. Simple commands make using Inflate easy: just one line in a button's script lets you add a help balloon. Any (unlikely) errors are returned in easily understood sentences. Advanced users have complete control over the help balloon, such as position of display, and balloon tip offset. In addition, a number of different balloon contents can be used: PICT resources, TEXT resources, and string resources. Version 1.2 is a complete re-write of the Inflate code, yielding improved performance and efficient code. Advanced users can now use short commands for Inflate (such as 'STR ' for 'Single string resource'). Also, the error handling has been vastly improved to make error checking much easier. Finally, a few small bugs have been fixed. Inflate is shareware; for more information on paying the small shareware fee, please see the stack. Commercial users should contact the author for more information.

title: Pythagoras
description: Training (Calculator needed) Language: German

title: Thai Tutor 2.2
description: ----------------- At last! The Sequel to Thai Tutor 1.1 -- Thai Tutor 2.2! This begins the lessons with the Thai alphabet. A comprehensive tutorial of Vowels, Consonants and the Tone Marks complete with full digitized sounds. **NOTE** This version comes with only limited sounds due to size limitations. The full version comes on two 800k disks with over 1 meg of digitized sounds! This demo is complete just minus about 70% of the sounds. Shareware fee is only $25. This is independent of Thai Tutor 1.1 but both are part of a series of Tutorials to teach an English speaking person the Thai (Thailand) language. Version 2.2 corrects a few bugs that have been found plus a few errors in the Thai spellings and some other misc. problems. This version also has a few more sounds than the previous one. Any questions or comments, please feel free to let me know! Dave

title: Basketball
description: The simple game of basketball done in HyperCard.

title: HTML Sketcher 1.1
description: ---------------- Create basic html documents for the World Wide Web with htmlSketcher. Using HyperCard, htmlSketcher helps you visualize the presentation of information one screen at a time. One click of a button then automatically produces rudimentary html pages, ready for further mark-up. Very simple, basic stack. Checked for Mac & HC viruses with Virex 5.6.

title: HyperGASP Color Tools & Textu...
description: This stack describes and demonstrates how color textures and patterns are supported in HyperGASP 2.3. HyperGASP is a multimedia authoring and auto-scripting extension for HyperCard. A larger, and more extensive, HyperGASP tour has been previously uploaded to this library. This stack describes one aspect of the HyperGASP interface -- its color tools -- in greater detail. This stack also includes: * 15 new color patterns (ppats) which can be used in any stack. * Instructions on how to design your own custom color patterns for use with HyperCard, using ResEdit. Checked for viruses with Disinfectant 3.5 before upload.

title: Outbreak!

It all starts with a monkey... *******BONUS OFFER******* If you e-mail me a comment, you'll get another animation free! More details inside! This file is self-extracting. (Phew!) This stack has been checked for Mac and HyperCard viruses with Virex 5.6

title: Piano Keys v 5.1.3
description: Fixes bug in old version 2.1. Read the description in that version because I don't feel like rewriting it. By the way, it is a synthesizer with a flute and piano. ------------------------- Previous version file description: The latest addition from Sure Death Software, the makers of Fryman, Icon Animation, and Hyper tic-tac-toe. This program is shareware, $3.50, but this is going to be the last creation of the Sure Death Software (A great company) if you guys don't pay your fee. I don't really care, I mean it is only $3.50, and I enjoy making games and programs, but; the group of three Deetokz, J Valjean1, and Xpress 911 are thinking about marketing are stacks professionally in bundles of disks. If you don't want us to do this, write one of us or even better hand over the shareware fee. Back to the stack. It is a piano/flute synthesizer (very educational and fun) that lets you create a song, play it back, save it, then play it back at a later date. Worth the 1 minute download, but I guess even a virus would be worth the download. This file has been checked by Norton Utilities, Sam AntiVirus, Virex, Vaccine, and Disinfectant and was found virus free. Write us.

title: Terminology Quiz 2.0
description: Quiz uses the Music Glossary as a database to create a quiz, or the teacher may create hir own new glossary of terms.

title: The Complete Works of Mozart ...
description: The Complete Works of Mozart 1.1 The Complete Works of Mozart is a set of four stacks which contain the entire Kochel catalog online. The Kochel catalog is the listing of Mozart's works as compiled by Ludwig von Kochel in 1869. This stack set contains complete information on the composition, also where and when it was written. This set also includes: - Works that were recently discovered and have not yet been assigned to the catalog. - Works that are still in question, as to whether or not they were indeed works of Mozart, or where they fit into the catalog numbering scheme. - Complete search capabilities. A must for ANY Mozart fan! Shareware, $5. Copyright 1993 Software Perspectives. All Rights Reserved.

title: The Hacker Crackdown
description: TYPE: Freeware This is the electronic version of Bruce Sterling's non-fiction book 'The Hacker Crackdown'. It is distributed on electronic services with the permission of the book's publisher. It describes in detail the events and individuals involved in the infamous 'Operation Sundevil' raids, Phrack magazine, and other events of the late 80s and early 90s.

title: The Rape of Lucrece
description: An annotated arrangement of the play The Rape of Lucrece, by William Shakespeare. This stack was authored by Mark Zimmermann, and is part of the Shakespeare Stack Project This stack has been released as Free Software under the GNU General Public License.

title: Twin Peaks
description: ----------------- 'Twin Peaks, the 1st Season' aka. 'The Twin Peaks bedside companion' is a HyperCard guide to the first 8 episodes (the 'TV Movie' and the 7 series episodes).

title: column() math functions
description: HyperCard stack demonstration stack and text file of CompileIt! code. This function performs statistical functions on tables of numerical data of unlimited size. A real life saver with stock trading prices and other large blocks of data.

title: Dead Shows
description: This is a database of Grateful Dead shows

title: Midi Metronome
description: Midi Metronome is a Hypercard stack which drives a midi sound generator (Sound Canvas) for the purpose of teaching proper timing to musicians and music students. A simple repeating drum pattern (boom/chuk) allows you to FEEL the beat compared to most metronomes with those wimpy 'clicks' which can never be heard over the sound of the instrument. Tempo's are fully adjustable by the student with a mouse click. If you do not have expensive midi equipment available but would reap the benefits of 'feeling' your metronome, drop me a note (no pun intended). I have created a series of metronome cassette tapes which can be played through any home stereo setup. Not as convenient and flexible as midi, but a lot cheaper! I used a Roland Sound Canvas SC-55, but the stack should work fine with other sound modules as well (kinda). It assumes you are running midi through the modem port at 1mhz. The port is reset when you first launch the stack so don't open the stack if you're connected to an on-line service! Enjoy! CIS: 75046,754

title: Moods & Conjunctions: Indra's Net III
description: Collocational procedures applied to three related texts. Published on disk, London: Wellsweep Press, 1993-94.

title: See The Sky Vol 1, 2, 3 & 4
description: See The Sky Vol 1, 2, 3 & 4 Adventure of SnowMan (all) (Xmas story!) All volumes play successively, so just start with Vol 1 to see the whole story.

title: Sun and Moon 3.0 Demo.sit
description: Welcome to the standalone Sun and Moon 3.0 Demo, which develops association and visual discrimination skills. Children find two objects that go together in the first activity. They point to the picture of the bird and the nest, for instance, rather than the car or the planet. In an alternate activity, students see a scene in which there are objects that don't belong. If they pick out all of the objects, they receive a reward! Sun and Moon encourages critical thinking, while appealing to children with its colorful graphics and sound. This demo version is fully functional, with sounds and animation, but only contains a sampling of the association and discrimination activities. Look for the other two titles in the Early Learning Series here online: Alphabet Zoo and Number Aquarium.

title: Word Count
description: Counts the number of unique words in a text file and builds a list of those words with their frequencies.

title: Astrophoto Exposure Stack v1.1
description: ----------------- This stack calculates correct shutter speeds for planetary photography. Just push buttons for the f-stop, ISO, and planet you wish to photograph and the stack calculates the proper exposure. Can also calculate exposures for phases of the Moon and solar eclipses. Scientific references included. Version 1.1 has improved logic and great pictures of the Sun, planets, and Moon. Free Trial. $5 Shareware.

title: Beginning Hypercard
description: ard. This is a stack for the novice that can be used as a 'help' stack. It's 'shareware' in that we'd like to see some of what you, the beginner have created. So share it with us!

title: Earth
description: It's about the destution of the earth. Don't forget to E-Mail Checked for Mac & HC viruses with Virex 5.6.

title: Lotto Math Tool
description: Test's yours or a program generated lotto number set from 1 up to 999 games, shows the statistics of matches, winnings. A well written stack, has a couple of 'easter eggs.'

title: Map Skills 1.0
description: Map Skills is a tutorial stack. Its main purpose is to teach symbol reading skills to 8-10 year olds. The stack utilizes eleven map symbols from an urban environment. This stack incorporates several interesting design features. It has a randomized reinforcement card that uses pop-up graphics and music. It uses a scorer with right, wrong, and percent fields. The design layouts of the card, maps, and questions are clean and efficient. Map Skills is a good model for tutorial stack creators as well. There are complete instructions included within the stack. Checked for Mac & HC viruses with Virex 5.6

title: The Multiplication Stack
description: I wrote this stack to help my own kids, Michael and Julia, learn their times tables but I thought others might find it useful too. If you want to modify it or see how it works, you'll have to unlock it.

title: North American Insect Collections 1.0
description: Developed at U-Michigan, this stack is a reference guide for insect lovers all over the United States. It lists places to borrow specimens, locations of collections and specialists. Allows personal notes to be added to the stack.

title: NoExit HyperCard Utility
description: No exiting or changing this stack! (unless you know the secret word is 'secret'!) This stack contains two simple handlers (scripts), designed to prevent unauthorized departure from (or changes to) your HyperCard stack. (If you need to ask why this would be desirable, you obviously have never dealt with the cleverness of young scholars in an academic setting!)

title: Roman Mythology Demo
description: This stack is a self-contained, fully operational part of a series of stacks that comprise a 'textbook' of classical mythology, known as HyperMyth. In this Roman Mythology stack you can read the myths of the Romans, see descriptions of the Roman deities, compare Roman and Greek gods, read about terms from Roman myths that are still used in English (such as 'cereal'), view pictures of constellations that come from Roman religion, hear pronunciations of names in the stories, look at family trees of the characters, and see an animated map of Aeneas' voyage. This stack is distributed as a 'demo' version of the entire HyperMyth program. Information on ordering the full version is included in the stack.

title: ShowMessage 1.0
description: Currently, there are two standard methods of displaying a message to the user in HyperCard without using external code: the message box and the answer dialog. However, the message box is terribly ugly, especially when you have put a lot of work into an elegent graphic interface. The answer dialog isn't always appropriate because it requires a user response, and it disappears before a script can continue to execute. Often what's needed is an elegent way of displaying a message, and the ability to control both invoking and hiding the message from within a script. My solution is ShowMessage. ShowMessage allows you to control both the display and disposal of a message window from within a script. This is accomplished entirely using HyperCard objects and HyperTalk--no externals are required. Once ShowMessage is set up in a stack, it's very easy to use.

title: Sokoban Level Editor 3.5
description: MacSokoban Level Editor lets you design your own levels and edit existing levels of Sokoban 3D, MacSokoban, a Mac- Sokoban module or Windows Sokoban.INI. Its interface is like any paint software you use. If you don't utilize Netscape, but the appl you have accept the GetURL command, you can use this stack. Other info in the stack's about button. Happy collection !

As a composer I do this quite a bit, and so I'm sure other composers and song writers, singers and other musicians will find it handy. I have also given this stack to a number of instrumental ensembles who submit a lot of tapes for grant proposals. It has allowed them to give a consistent, professional look to their presentations. FEATURES: - On Line Help. - Settings for 'normal' sized cassette boxes as well as the newer 'slim' boxes. - A pop-up menu of your available fonts, as well as 'number wheel' font size and line height adjustments. - Card cloning, so you can duplicate an existing entry when you need to make changes but want to keep your original. - Card protection. - Dolby B or C indicator. - Organize your labels using keywords by selecting appropriate text from the label itself, then compile the list. I've included labels for several works of my own as examples of how this stack can work.

title: Learn KANA, version 3.0
description: ----------------- This update will be provided to owners of previous versions FREE of charge. If you are an owner and your address has changed since you purchased Learn KANA, send me your change of address and I ll send you a new disk. Sidney L. Webb 1113 Fincastle Rd. Lexington, KY 40502 CHANGES 1. Menus have been improved 2. The single character test has been improved. 3. Scores can now be saved. From 100 to 300 records can be stored and printed. By selecting a record a student can review troublesome kana and retest only those characters. 4. The user may now turn off the opening sound as well as other sounds available. WHAT'S IN THE COMPLETE VERSION Kana are presented four different ways -- hiragana and katakana are complete on one card, hiragana and katakana presented on two cards each, both the kanas presented in groups of five, each character of both kanas presented as single flashcards. The style of the kana is differs from one presentation to another so that students will become familiar with both typeset characters as well as brush characters. The stack is complete with music and pronunciations of the characters. WHAT'S NOT HERE This Learn KANA library is not complete. Hiragana have been removed. Pictures depicting some of the Japanese culture have also been removed. Otherwise it is all here...all the katakana, the options, and the record keeping and re-testing features.

title: BasicDB XCMDs 1.0 Demo
description: This is a fully functioning demo of my new database external set calld BasicDB. It is a demo because write access to database files will expire 14 days after it was created. Notice I said, write access. If you wish to purchase this product, please read the information on the first card. BasicDB is basic. A BasicDB file can contain any number of records. Each record, however, has a fixed structure of the following: 1. A Numeric key (32 bit long integer) 2. A String key of up to 31 characters 3. Textual data limited only by diskspace. (You must also have enough RAM to work with that data [read/write]) 4. Record ID that is unique to that item and is totally controlled by the system. BasicDB does not have any indexing or anything fancy. It does have searching. You can search for an equality on the numeric key or the string key or a 'contains' search on the text data. Here is a list of the calls. BasicDBCreate BasicDBNewItem BasicDBSetItem BasicDBGetItem BasicDBFindItem BasicDBCompact BasicDBNumItems BasicDBGetIndex I developed BasicDB becuase I wanted to store the data I was working with outside of HyperCard, but I didn't need any sort of fancy stuff and I wanted to store lots of data inside a single record. Brian Blood Maverick Enterprises ---- From SIG Leader: These XCMDs were all created with CompileIt!. Note that no source code is provided.

title: Consensus Timers 2.03
description: the enclosed timer application, consensus timers, was originally an alternative to juggling handfuls of digital kitchen timers. now improved and fully documented, it includes drag-able progress bars and supports any number of timers running simultaneously. easy to use, reliable, and unobtrusive in busy and distracting situations, cs-timers can run in background behind other HyperCard stacks and Mac applications. active timers measure real-world elapsed time accurately, even if you quit HyperCard or shutdown and restart. each timer can range from seconds to many years, with optional auto-total/distribution of subtimers for planning and scheduling. reminder alarms may also be set by scripts in other stacks. for more details see the read-me text, and the stacks include template cards and full documentation in hypertext. this is shareware, inexpensive, small, just plain HyperCard, yet professionally produced and of commercial quality. imho, it's an impressive example of HyperCard's capabilities, and you're welcome to read my HyperTalk source code (more than 80k). consensus timers is fully functional (not a demo), and needs no special installation. i hope people will try it and find it useful.

description: ----------------- Yes, you too can now sound like a big-time PHILOSOPHER with the 'Horizon of Hermeneutic Ontology Philosophical Phrase Generator'.
