title: Timed-Out v1.11
description: Version1.11.....January 11, 1993 ___________________ Even though HyperCard is a handy, simple-to-work-with database, it cannot be shared by multiple users on a network. When the standard System 7 sharing is used, I believe a stack open on one computer can be accessed as read only by another computer. Worse yet, using Appleshare on a server, a person trying to access a stack on the server that is already in use gets a message something like 'This stack is busy.' I hope this can be remedied in the next update of HyperCard. One way to help work around this problem, when you need to have multiple stack users, is to have the database stack automatically quit afer a defined period of time. This stack contains a simple script at the stack level to do just that. After 5 minutes, it will show a hidden field stating...... 'If you need another 5 minutes use-time, hit the 'Reset Use-time' button, otherwise this stack will quit in 1 minute to make it available for other users.' The stack's 'Reset Use-time' button can be used to respond to or preempt the inevitable warning. Copy the script, button, and hidden background field (named 'Warning') to your HyperCard database stack, and change the timing to whatever is reasonable for typical use of your database. If you have other solutions, I think a lot of Mac users would be appreciative. Robert Hangebrauck...RobertPH on American Online....Internet: robertph@aol.com

title: About XCMD 1.1
description: About XCMD 1.1 Sunday, June 21, 1998 If you knock yourself out creating a stack that is rich in features and possessing of great depth, you begin to think of your stack as an application, with HyperCard being the 'engine' that drives it. If that is the case then you should be able to give your stack credit by placing 'About xxxx...' under the Apple in the same fashion as is done by all applications. The arguments of About XCMD are: Pass one parameter: About 'About this stack...' This replaces the 'About HyperCard...' menu item with one of your own. Pass two parameters: About 'About this stack...','Stack Help...' This also adds a second custom menu choice just below the 'About...' item. If you do not wish to change the 'About HyperCard...' item then pass a SPACE as the first parameter: About ' ','Stack help...' -- remember to pass a SPACE as the 1st parameter! Pass NO parameters: About This restores the Apple menu to its original state. It doesn't matter whether you customized the menu with one or two items. Use this form in your closeStack handler. This stack includes the source code to the XCMD.

title: BitXFCN
description: ----------------- Usage: NewNr = Bit (Operation, Number [, base [,Number2]] ) Bit accepts 2-4 parameters: - a string with the number signifying the operation - a string with the number - a string with the base used (optional) - a string with an optional second number Bit returns a string of the result ______________________________________ Operations: 0 NOP 1 Rotate Left * 2 Rotate Right * 3 Shift Left * 4 Shift Right * 5 AND ** 6 OR ** 7 XOR ** 8 NOT 9 NEGATE 10 Base2Base *** * These operations take the optional second number as a parameter, signifying the nr of bits a shift or rotation should run ** These operations take a second Operand to indicate the nr of bits (default is 1). *** In the form: bits (Base2Base, Nr1, Base1, Base2) Default for base2 is Hexadecimal If the Base is not specified or left out ( via an empty string between two commas), it is assumed to be decimal. Bit only accepts unsigned integers (as the minus sign is an operator in reality and we do not process operators). The range is 0..&FFFFFFFF, the full longint range. This should be sufficient for most applications. Base can be from 2 to theoretically maxint, but the charset will limit this practically to 10 26 chars for coding a digit. So the biggest possible base would be 36. There is no range checking (Which should not be of any problem, provided I did program this correctly (it has been tested with range checking) and there is no overflow checking. This means the Hypercard programmer has to be aware of that. ________________________________ Send comments to: Stephan Meyn 315 Keizan Heights 1-30-3 DenenChofu Ota-Ku, Tokyo 145 Japan or Email to: STMEYN (BIX, TWICS) (X.400 DFN

title: getMouseLoc XFCN
description: This is one of many many fabulous external functions (XFCN) and external commands (XCMD) by Mike Bailey (TuikBlade). The stack shows you the location of the mouse relative to the card and to the screen (meaning that if you put it all the way into the upper-left corner, it'll return 0,0). The call is simple, and I've found use for it. just call getMouseLoc() (pass a 'g' and it'll give you the global coordinates [getmouseLoc(g)]). E-mail him with any questions or comments. Oh, and as far as he's told me it's freeware. Look for his collection of Xodus Xternals, uploaded soon (as soon as he feels there are enough to justify uploading) to America Online. Do try to tell him if you put this on your site, or if you include it in a shareware/freeware package of any sort. I'm sure he'd love to find out where his stuff has gone. -------------- Staff Note: The card and global mouseLoc is also available via HC scripting ... however, you may find this external an easier solution. Checked for Viruses with Disinfectant & MerryXmas Vaccine

title: Scientific Bibliography Stack...
description: OPTIONAL: Access to SilverPlatter Medline SYSTEM 7: Compatible TYPE: Freeware stack Author's email address: lho@biobase.aau.dk Author's description: This stack is my first go at making a HyperCard stack. I have written it, because I needed a database for my scientific articles. The stack may also be used to keep a database of your books, etc. I have written an import function in the stack, this makes it possible to import downloaded from Silverplatter Medline. If you know a little about 'programming' in HyperCard, it is very easy to make it import other formats. Feel free to use this stack and give copies away, but don't charge money for it!!! You may also modify your personal copy, customising it to your needs. Please give the original copy to other interested Mac users. Input: You can either type the references into the correct field yourself or use the import-function. When typing the references yourself, the tab key will move the insertion point to the next field. The import-function is designed for Silverplatter Medline. You will find the function in the menu bar under 'x' (sigma). Each field, in a reference to be imported, is separated by a return and each new reference is separated by three returns. Further help on the format to be found in the stack. I have included a file downloaded from Medline, where you can see the format. You can also try importing this file with the import-function (it should work). If you want to import text from another database, it is very easy to modify the script yourself. If you are not familiar with HyperCard scripting, you are welcome to drop me a line. Output: You can transfer the references to a text file with the export-function in the menu bar under 'x' (sigma). Each field will be separated by a tab and each reference with a return. You can of course also modify this in any way you may like. I think this is very usefull for generating a bibliogaphy, if you are writing articles yourself. You can also print the references, just like any other HyperCard stack, by choosing 'Print report' from the menu bar.

title: DragWindow
description: I wrote this WIndowDrag XCMD as an exercise in really simple CompileIt! coding. No parameters are passed to this XCMD. Simply include it anywhere in a mouseDown handler and the mouse will drag a gray outline of the window around your monitor(s) and redraw it at the correct location on mouseUp It uses the Mac DragWindow toolbox call to work so it works well across multiple monitors. Works with HyperCard, SuperCard or WindowScript. You can design your own 'fake' title bar and use this instead of the ones the named products usually supply. Use 'hide titlebar' to hide HyperCard's title bar. Only the version of this stack uploaded to the CompileIt! SIG Library contains the CompileIt! code for this XCMD. Have fun! Gary Stanoulis CompileIt! SIG Leader

title: HyperDiary 2.0 Demo
description: HyperDiary is the best digital diary available. Here are some of the many features: (This demo version will allow you to create only 7 cards before you register at which time you will be sent the 'unlimited full-use' version of HyperDiary. * Dates are formatted for the turn of the century! So if you enter a date such as 4/15/25, that will be seen as April 15, 2025 and NOT April 15, 1925! * Word and phrase indexing. You can enter a word or phrase, and HyperDiary will search the entire diary for your matching entries, and list all the dates where you've entered that word or phrase. Then, all you have to do is to click on a date in the list, and HyperDiary will jump to the page where you originally entered the word or phrase and select the exact entry on the page! * Full color for HyperCard 2.3 or greater. * A full 'point-and-click' help function. All you have to do is to click a button, and HyperDiary suddenly goes into 'balloon' style help where all you have to do is to click an area of HyperDiary to find out how to use anything you see. * Full password protection. * Fast-Hide for instant security should someone enter the room. * A global 'replace' function. This will allow you, for example, to replace a single word or phrase if you've been mispelling it for awhile. * A complete color screen saver (for HyperCard 2.3 or newer) at the click of a button. Staff Note: Scanned for viruses with Disinfectant, MerryXmas Vacine.

description: What is the meaning of life? Mankind has searched for the answer to that one for years. Many of us make it our lifelong occupation. Some of us are lucky enough to find some degree of satisfaction in the answers we get. Others of us never come close. In any event, pursuit of the answer to this question can be very rewarding. A good foundation in the concepts presented here can contribute to your success, no matter what endeavors you attempt. What is 'The Meaning of Life?' In this case it is a HyperCard Stack which explores the key philosophical concepts which codify the thoughts of some of the greatest thinkers in history. The stack uses the user-friendly HyperCard interface to explore key concepts, the history of philosophy, key documents, biographies, and quotes from the greats on the meaning of life and human nature.

title: Down, A Novel
description: DOWN is fantasy/magic realism. The story opens in the ancient city of Levinfield, home of a civilization so old that their libraries collapse beneath the weight of their books, where the monetary system is based on poetry and . History follows a regular cycle, from industrial peak to decadent trough and back again, and the city is now in the middle of a quiet era of contented peace. Few people leave the city, as all their needs are catered for by the leftovers from the last industrial age, the automated Factories which can manufacture anything required and the antique mines where fossilized machines are dug from beneath the ruins. Lang, however, has been reading. In the libraries he uncovers references to a great forest to the West, and a desire stirs in him to explore, leave the safe streets and venture out into the deserted countryside. One legend in particular catches his interest. In the last days of the previous Industrial Age a truckdriver disappeared while passing through the forest on his way to the outlying villas of the rich Factory-owners. Since then his ghost was said to haunt the road, ceaselessly searching for a way out, a pale wraith in white tee-shirt and navy shorts, wandering through the trees crying "Map! Maaap!", searching for directions. Lang vows to track the ghost down and lay it to rest. His friends try and prevent him leaving. Subconsciously they fear he is the first sign of the next aggressive wave of greed, desire and development. But he is determined. He readies his rusted trailbike, acquires a longbow for protection, and sets out. Deep in the heart of the forest, in a castle that for millennia lay buried beneath the mold, Catherine toils obsessively at her life's work. She is a descendant of the Factory-owners of old, run-away from her parents crumbling villa to the West of the forest. She records on tape the words spoken by parrots that nest in the trees - words of a forgotten language of great power. Passed down through generations of birds, this language, (when the words are spoken in the right order), can harness strong magical energy. Catherine records and catalogues and files the words of power and never really knows why. As Lang unknowingly approaches her castle he encounters strange things. By night he hears the ghostly cry, "Maap!", and sees a white shape moving through the fog, until one night when the air is clear and he sees the ghost for what it really is - an albino deer, it's coat sparkling with phosphorescent spores, that bleats the mournful cry as it grazes. He decides to hunt it down and carry the skin back to the city to dispel the myth. Catherine, her senses amplified by magic, senses the deer's death. She knew it well, it was almost her pet. In her anger she decides to punish Lang in the worst way possible. She will create a void, a bottomless hole, an empty universe, and cast him in. For eternity he will float through space, alive, aware but unable to escape, lost in nothingness. As Lang casually heads back to Levinfield, the white pelt lashed across his pack, she works to prepare the spell, cutting and splicing tape in a mad frenzy. The trap works. One moment Lang is walking through the forest, the next he finds himself suspended in the highest room in the castle, an inky black well of nothing beneath his feet, Catherine standing before him triumphant. And then...? Read on, to find out how they both come to be trapped in the void, how together they escape from the impossible, how they come to find themselves in inner-city Sydney, in a world they have created from their dreams, a world corrupted by their antagonism and twisted by their desperation, a world which, finally, they have to decide either to allow to continue or to destroy utterly.

description: ----------------- XTrace and XTrap are XCMD/XFCN debugging utilities. They can be used to see what callbacks an XCMD/XFCN is making into HyperCard, and to change the results returned to the XCMD/XFCN. (Throughout this text XCMDs and XFCNs are referred to simply as XCMDs. XTrace and XTrap work with either). >>XTrace description XTrace loads and executes any XCMD and traps the callbacks the XCMD makes to HyperCard. It builds a list of the callbacks and their parameters, and puts the list into the HyperTalk global variable 'callbacks.' This list can be displayed to show what callbacks the XCMD made. The callbacks are executed, and the behavior of the XCMD is not affected. >>XTrap description XTrap is similar to XTrace, but rather than making a list of the callbacks, it sends a HyperTalk message to the current card with the callback information, and lets you write a handler to intercept the callback and even change the value returned. For example, suppose you use an XCMD that gets the name of the current stack and prints the stack name on a report, and you'd like it to print something else. You can trap the callback that asks for the stack name [EvalExpr('the short name of this stack')] and return something else. After using XTrace to see what callbacks an XCMD makes, you can use XTrap to change the results and modify the XCMD's behavior (or get more control over what the XCMD does to your stack).

title: Project Management
description: ----------------- 'Projects' is an incomplete stack. It's a demonstration of a HyperTalk technique that relys on the presence of three externals by various authors. The three stacks that contain those externals may also be down loaded from AOL. The names of those files (on AOL) are: 1) 'FileName 1.3.2.sit' by Steve Maller 2) 'XNET PowerList' distributed by XNET or 'HyperList 3.2' by James L. Paul (on CIS only so far as I know) 3) 'PopUpMenu example.sit' by Andrew Gilmartin PowerList can be downloaded from AOL, and HyperList 3.2 is available on CIS. The scripting for the two is different, but both versions are in the 'Projects' button script. The PowerList version is functioning and the HyperList version is commented out. I use HyperList myself because it can return an index number directly rather than requiring another couple of lines of code to calculate it. Qualifications aside, here is what 'Projects' does. It enables a pop up menu with a list of 'projects', ie. any stack that you may be working on or that you use frequently. This helps me get to the stuff I'm working on without cluttering up my Home screen with a lot of buttons. You access this menu by pressing down on the 'Projects' button. The empty popup contains two selections and a disable line. The selections are 'New Project' and 'Delete Project. When you select 'New Project' you get an open file dialog (courtesy of the fileName XFCN) that will show only stacks. If you select one, you will be asked to provide a name for the menu name of the project. The stacks name will be the default answer. When you click the OK button on that dialog, that stack will be added to the popup menu. At that point you can pull down that menu, select the stack you want, and you're there. It's not as versitile as HyperCards built in button link facility in that you can't specify a card within the stack. If you need that feature, use a regular button. This is much more compact as I noted above, and more dynamic. Deleting projects is just as easy as adding them. Select the 'Delete Project' option and you'll get a list dialog of the current active projects. Select one, and it's gone from the menu. You can Cancel of course, or add the stack again if you change your mind. Download the stacks listed above and paste their externals into your Home stack. I think you HyperHackers out there find this as useful as I have. Enjoy. Ed Nutter - AOL EdNutter - CIS 76167,102

title: Color Pictures
description: An XCMD to show PICT files or resources in HyperCard's window. As well as a bonus set of scripts to make pseudo picture objects. Way back in 1989 I had a color macintosh and HyperCard. No one was doing color for HyperCard so I took it apon myself to put something together. At about the same time Stan Gilbert came out with TKShowPict. His method was quite a bit better than my own so I stole it. What made his idea so important was NOT how to get at picture on the screen. That part was easy, but how to tell if it had been erased. While I will cover this in the background scripts and the script of the card button on the next card I did want to make sure credit was given where credit was due. Explore the scripts to get a good feel on how to fool folks into thinking HC really does color. Why not use his XCMD? Well Stan Gilberts was what I needed initally it had a two flaws: 1) I could not define a rectangle in which to place the picture. 2) I could not read in a PICT file. I needed to do both. So in the great tradition of necessity birthing invention. I took my ideas and merged them with his idea and came up with my own XCMD. Synatax ShowPicture [/], [/,[/],[Use Picture Rect ] Looks like ShowPicture 'Logo','0,0' Draw the PICT resource at card location 0,0. ShowPicture 'Bogus HD:Some Folder:My Picture', rect of cd btn 3, file Draw PICT file 'My Picture' at 'Bogus HD:Some Folder' into the rect of cd btn 3. ShowPicture 'Logo', rect of btn 2, True Draws PICT resource Logo at the upper right hand corner of btn 2 and returns the PICT size in the result. ShowPicture 'MyHD:Logo', rect of bg btn 2, File, True and ShowPicture 'MyHD:Logo', rect of bg btn 2, True, File Draws PICT File Logo at the upper right hand corner of btn 2 and returns the PICT size in the result. Software and Hardware Reqirements HyperCard version 2.0 and system 4.0 or greater. If there is great enough outcry I can re-rev it so that it will work with HC 1.2.x. Justification The ShowPicture XCMD places a great deal of the burden on HyperTalk programmer to 'know' the state of the HC window at any given time. Is this fair? I am not sure. It works fine for me and a few other people I know who have used this XCMD. It is very fast, the code is only 2000 bytes. Most of the code is used for parsing the XCMD arguments (and as such most is skipped). The only other way to keep track of when HC redraws it's window is to patch a trap or two. This has been done by the BungDabba folks. And if you want to worry less about when HC redraws over your color pictures their product, which is free, may interest you. If you want blazing speed this will do it for you. Even though it will look kinda tacky sometimes.

This new version has just been uploaded because of B&W cursor error which has been corrected. This is a great XCMD and you will find vast usefulness for it- so what are you waiting for there is the 'Download Now' button? This is shareware- $5. NOTE: Please do not copy the Install XCMD which is designed specifically for installing ColorCurs into other stacks. Here are the parameters: ---------------------- = Explanation of parameter [] = Optional parameter ColorCurs(,[]) or ColorCurs( ) or ColorCurs (must have quotes and be in proper case but you can add on to end - such as: 'Help!') title: DefaultCurs 1.01
description: ----------------- DefaultCurs is a utility for changing the standard HyperCard cursor in any stack from the Hand to any other predefined cursor. Unlike other utilities, such as the 'changecursor' XCMDs that change the cursor only until reset by HyperCard, DefaultCurs changes the standard cursor permanently for any stack in which it is installed. The effects of DefaultCurs can be reversed by removing defaultCurs' resources from the stack. DefaultCurs is useful, for example, if you want to use the arrow as the standard/default cursor in a stack, thus making it conform more closely to traditional Mac interface guidelines. The demo stack uses the arrow as the default cursor, but includes instructions for making any other cursor the default. Note: this does not work in HC 2.0, and probably won't in the future either (the docs warn about this).

title: Scavenger Hunt
description: WELCOME TO SCAVENGER HUNTÂ©! This software was created with children in mind. The program is specifically created for kindergarten through second graders; those who are working on reading skills. It not only offers reading and matching exercises throughout the basic game, but each room of the house has a "hidden" activity that helps underline reading disciplines: â¢ in one room, children play "dress up" and learn clothing names â¢ in another room, children follow a recipe to create a new treat â¢ another room offers a book that keeps stealing letters from a story â¢ in other rooms, there is opportunity to match tools, fold laundry, and try out interesting pets â¢ and other rooms offer fun sounds, games and other spelling feats! Of course, we can't tell you where any of these rooms are, nor how to get into them, that's part of the fun! The basic "game" as you enter the house is to go from room to room using the "map" provided on the main page. Try to find objects that BEGIN with the letters on your "list" found with the map. You will only be able to take one object back with you from any room, and once you make a letter match, that room will be "locked." (This feature can be turned off if you wish, by clicking "off" the button "Use room covers" below the house map.) You have to find ONE object for each letter to complete the game, and there are literally HUNDREDS of objects all over the house!! If you wish to remove a word you found, click on that word in your list and it will be removed (and if you are playing with room covers, the room will be unlocked so you can go back and find other options.) You may want to "explore" before you begin, so you have a feel for where things are. The game will ONLY speak the first letters, not the entire word. This encourages children and adults to work together and "read" the game. There are fun side tracks all through the house. Check doors, cupboards and piles of things, you never know what you'll find! But most of all, have fun!! If your version is titled "MiniScavenger HuntÂ©", then your program is made to fit on the MacClassic screen (9"). The original "Scavenger HuntÂ©" was designed for the larger screen. If you have the wrong version, simply write or call ImagiNation Enterprises and we will gladly send you the other version. This program is SHAREWARE, so if you decide to keep it, please send the amount of $5.00 to ImagiNation Enterprises at the following address: ImagiNation Enterprises 463 N 800 E American Fork, Utah 84003-1919 (801) 763-0682 This program is actually a HyperCardâ¢ stack written in HyperCardâ¢ 2.2, and requires HyperCardâ¢ 2.0 or newer to run it. If you have any questions or suggestions, please send them to ImagiNation Enterprises! All rights reserved. Â©1996 by ImagiNation Enterprises

title: Space-Aligned Columns
description: This utility converts tab-delimited text from a database or word processor into space-separated columns, with each tab replaced by the correct number of (monospace-font) spaces to align the columns. The original purpose of this utility was to be able to use the "PRE" HTML tag to properly display columns of tab-delimited data on a web browser, but it is useful for any situation where tabs must be replaced. This is freeware. First, paste the tabbed text into this field (the tabs will show as spaces, but they're still there). Then click the "No. of Columns" button and enter the number, then click the "Widths" button which will ask you for column widths (number of characters), which should be entered separated by commas. Then click "Justify" if you want anything other than left justified columns. Each column can have its own justification; left, center, or right. Then click "Add space" if you need to insert extra space between any columns (such as a left-justified column next to a right-justified column). Finally "Convert" will do the conversion, take you to the next page, and select it all in preparation for copying and pasting. If the total number of characters in each row is wider than the window, it won't look good yet - you have to paste it into a wide-enough window to see the true results. As is often the case, creating this solution took longer than putting in spaces by hand in the original situation. But, if I ever have to do it again, now I have a tool. And so do you.

title: ReadCat XFCN.SIT
description: ----------------- The ReadCat XFCN will give you handy access to much of the information in the floppy disk catalogs to use as you see fit in your stacks. You may display the catalog brazenly as I do in my stack, Auto Floppy Log 3.5, or you may elect to protect your users from this unpleasantness by using the information as a skeleton for fancy graphic representations. The ReadCat XFCN may be used in several modes. The simplest is a HyperTalk statement such as 'put ReadCat() into catbox.' This will cause HyperCard to prompt the user to insert a floppy disk which will then be read. The catalog will end up in the variable 'catbox' or any other container, in the following format: Line 1: The Volume Name of The Floppy Disk Lines 2 to (The Number of Files and Folders on The Disk): There will be 7 comma delimited items in each line as follows: Item 1: Will be either 'File' or 'Fldr' depending on which it is. Item 2: Will contain the short Date Last Modified of the file. Item 3: The Size of the File in KiloBytes. Item 4: The File Name. Item 5: The Directory ID number (see below). Item 6: The Parent ID number (Yup, parents too, see below). Item 7: The File Type, like 'APPL', 'TEXT', 'STAK'

title: Audio Sequencer!
description: Audio Sequencer is a HyperCard stack created as a poor-man's sequencer. With no interface with MIDI or anything else for that matter, it's accuracy is partially dependent on your reflexes. Designed to play short sounds, such as sound effects (gunshots, etc.), it functions very well as a low end sequencer. Okay, maybe it's not really a sequencer, but it does have the ability to play a sound (or a series of sounds) at the exact moment you want it to. Designed with the synching to video in mind, the standard time unit is one frame of video (1/30th of a second). Each card has a time ruler of two seconds, with sixty units on the ruler. A sound can be played on any one of those units. Concievably, you could play a different sound on every frame (who knows what it would sound like though!) You can have as many cards as you want, each representing two seconds. Or you can have one card represent several seconds. You can have as many sounds play over as long of time as you want (memory permitting). Actually, the time limit is 59:59:29, being 59 minutes, 59 seconds, and 29 frames. This hasn't been tested to work though (who has the time?) I've even surprised myself on this stack, it works really well and doesn't cost much (an e-mail registers you)! So why not give it a try all you home video and audio buffs, this may be just what you need to give some life to your masterpieces!

title: HyperCard Desktop Pub. Sample...
description: SchoolHouse Mac usually uploads to the MED forum but we uploaded this sampler here because the stack it represents has material of interest to HyperCard newcomers. Desktop Publishing with HyperCard Sampler contains samples of the graphics, projects, ideas and articles available in the nearly 1500k stack from SchoolHouse Mac. This particular sampler has a little less useful clipart than some of the samplers we've uploaded, but we wanted to give you an idea of topics, projects and tutorials, the larger stack contains. The complete stack incorporates, but goes far beyond, two project ideas we uploaded earlier. They are 'Awesome Tickets' and 'Notes and Memos'. They are still available in the MED forum. Teachers Desktop Publishing with HyperCard came about because of HyperCard's power and flexibility. The combination of graphic and writing tools along with the capability of creating stacks of varying sizes and printing in different sizes make HyperCard an excellent, basic desktop publishing program for teachers. Teachers Desktop Publishing with HyperCard stack is a departure from our other SuperClipArt stacks in that it combines about 400 pieces of clipart, borders, and backgrounds, with approximately 14 simple desktop publishing project ideas for personal use and use with elementary and middle school students. Complete instructions for these projects and working with HyperCard in connection with them are included. We included some simple suggestions for creating your own art and working with HyperCard's graphic tools as well. The complete stack serves as clipart resource, project idea source and HyperCard tutorial all in one. (Note: The intent is not to create a complete HyperCard tutorial, but the stack will help newcomers to HyperCard learn to use some of its features.) The sampler contains complete information about the stack and ordering. If you've any questions or comments, please contact me on AOL or write me at SchoolHouse Mac. Charles Doe SchoolHouse Mac 5236 Coats Grove Rd. Hastings, MI 49058

title: Details 1.2.1.SIT
description: ----------------- Version 1.2.1 is a BUG fix!!! Sorry to all who got the bad one. This stack will set you free to roam the inner reaches of any stack. It does most of the dirty work for you and leaves the fun exploring to you. I designed this stack to help me learn a lot more about the stacks that I was getting from the usual sources (paid for of course!!). However, with each stack I looked into, the more frustrated I became because I had to do a bunch of searching to find out how things worked. Then came an article in MacUser. Eureka! Part of my dreams were answered. No, I did not meet the woman I would marry, but I found a script that would help me look at a stack's inner workings. Unfortunately, I had to print the results the script put out and then go into the analyzed stack to do my search - even that seemed confusing. But I wanted more! (Don't they always want more??) Thus was born the thought of this stack. It has saved me a lot of headaches (and created a few of its own!). It is not the easiest stack to learn, but once you have used it, it becomes necessary if you want to learn some of the tricks people are using in their stacks without lots of random and frustrating exploration. I recommend you read the HELP card first??? Click on any '?' icon for help. Don't be afraid to LEARN!!!! I think there are several bugs that are still in the scripts, so please don't swear at me if it doesn't work properly. I'm not perfect either! I'm working on them! Let me know if you find bugs!

description: The purpose of The HyperCard FAQ is to provide information and answer basic questions about HyperCard; its scripting language, HyperTalk; and various resources and products relating to HyperCard.

title: HCtoWWW 1.01
description: HCtoWWW is an HyperCard to HTML Translator. It allows to translate all text contained in a stack to HTML page(s) with many options including styles, HTML hypertext links, URL detection, automatic Titles, T.O.C., image tags and more.

title: HyperDecisionTree-2.0.5
description: This is HyperDecisionTree 2.0.5 which fixes some bugs from the previous version. HyperDecisionTree is a non-inferential expert system shell which allows an expert to create a decision tree of their knowledge and then save the decision tree as a separate stack in order to give to users in need of the expertise contained within the stack. The stack primarily serves as an overview of the decision tree, allowing the expert to follow questions and answers down the road to specific conclusions. The expert can even display graphics as part of a conclusion. Troy Kelley

title: HyperEnigma 1.1
description: Low- (or medium-, if you're feeling generous) -security encryption algorithm with a pretty decent interface.

title: HyperGrab 1.0
description: HyperGrab 1.0 stack, by Erik Marcus, free. This utility copies out the card and background text fields of every card in a HyperCard stack, and labels the text according to the card and field it came from. You can also select ranges of cards from which to copy text. The text is then saved as a text file.

title: HyperKeys (Music & Sound)
description: Freeware version 1.0 HyperKeys Keyboard Music Writer allows you to write songs in hyperTalk format and save them to documents to be listened to or edited later. The Music Documents included with HyperKeys must be stored in the SAME folder as the HyperCard application itself. If they are not stored in that folder, then you will not be able to load the Music document (version 2.0 of HyperKeys will allow you to save to and open the documents from any location. These changes will be made to version 2.0, and all registered users get one free upgraded version). Using TeachText or HyperKeys Music Analyzer / Reader* (To be released following version 2.0) you can open the music documents and copy the song to be pasted into a HyperCard script (if you are a HyperCard Scipter). *HyperKeys Music Analyzer/Reader will be available at 25% off to anyone who has purchased from High Friction the HyperKeys version 2.0 software. HyperKeys Analyzer / Reader will be set to only work with HyperKeys version 2.0 files, to prevent pirating. Sorry if this security seems a bit tight. KEEP PLAYING THE GAMES! PS. If you aren't going to get the HyperKeys version 2.0 software, but can think of some improvements that might cause you to buy it, E-Mail me at HFGames

title: Internet Tour
description: InterNet Tour by NSF Network Service Center (good)

title: IsNumber XFCN.sit
description: ----------------- IsNumber XFCN returns whether a given string consists entirely of math-suitable numbers, including minus sign and decimal point. Returns 'True' for a numeric string, or 'False' if there are any non-math characters. Much faster than plain HyperTalk or SuperTalk. The XFCN performed 1000 iterations on a test string in 12 seconds, compared to nearly 2 minutes with plain HyperTalk. Can also be used to test for alpha characters, which would be the opposite result. Non-commercial use of this XFCN is free. =George=

title: Keyboard Stack
description: A simple stack that prompts the user to type a key, then catches keydown events and give appropriate feedback. I made it for a client with special access needs and afterward, thought I might build it up into a full-blown keyboarding program. I never found the time (or had the interest). Still, it's a nice little program for beginning the process of learning the keyboard. Those who can guess the voice in the stack are Mac/HyperCard old-timers. By the way, I have HIS permission to use it. If you find this stack useful and would like a list of our other stacks, please download the document Poor Richard's Software Catalog. If you'd like to see all of our offerings on AOL, use Quickfinder to find my screen name: rwanderman. Checked for Mac & HC viruses with Virex 5.6

title: Track Mutual Funds
description: If you track mutual funds using AOL's Stock Portfolio Display this stack will record the prices of the funds you track by date. Will show you the dates the High and Low occured. Will also graph the funds performance and you can print the graph with a button 'click'.

title: Workstation
description: ----------------- A hypercard finder

title: Back to Canterbury
description: A role-playing game that requires students to step into the shoes of various characters from The Canterbury Tales and successfully complete a series of "quests."

title: Letter Learner
description: An introduction to neural networks. Learns to recognize letters that you draw by hand, using the mouse.

title: List Disk Directory XFCN 1.21
description: ----------------- Version 1.21 - Bug with globals and A4 register fixed. This caused odd problems within Hypercard. If you are using 1.20 please replace it with 1.21. This XFCN will put a sorted list of files contained on a volume with their file info into a global variable in HyperCard. The list can be sorted by file name, file type or creator. This sample stack displays the first 394 file names in a card field. If there are more than that they are in the global variable but one card field can only hold 29996 bytes and that's 394 lines X 76 bytes maximum in this case. The delimiter between fields as well as the delimiter between records can be set in the call to allow for different preferences in how the information is used. Bob

title: Messy House
description: An educational game teaching users basic mouse skills as they learn to tidy various rooms.

title: Minuet Builder
description: Minuet Builder is a HyperCard interpretation of 'Musikalisches Wurfelspiel', a musical game that is widely attributed to Mozart. It contains over 759,499 billion possible minuets

title: Mission Get Home
description: Simple adventure game.

title: Number Aquarium 3.0 Demo.sit
description: Welcome to the standalone Number Aquarium 3.0 Demo, a fun way to help youngsters learn the basics of writing and counting their numbers. Number Aquarium integrates sound and animation to teach number formation and recognition to early education students. In the first of two activities, animals of the sea show how to draw the numbers. For instance, Pamela Penguin demonstrates how to draw the number four. The child can then use the mouse or other pointing device (such as the TouchWindow-TM or certain adaptive devices) to draw the number, formed on screen by IntelliTrace-TM. The second activity emphasizes counting skills. Each animal presents a number of objects to be counted. As the child points to each object, Number Aquarium counts along with the child. This demo version is fully functional, with sounds and animation, but only contains the numbers 1 through 5. Look for the other two titles in the Early Learning Series here online: Alphabet Zoo and Sun and Moon.

title: Pager Alarms (v0.9)
description: Page nearly any numeric pager from your Mac; messages/alarms coded according to a built-in schedule; requires HyperCard 2.x, a modem; future versions will incorporate voicemail, alpha-numeric paging.

title: KARATEstack 1.6d
description: ial art, you'll find that this is a 'must have' for your Macintosh. For those not interested in martial arts, but want to see the potential of Hypercard as a learning aid, or how it can best implement Quicktime movies, KARATEstack is an absolute must! It shows the true potential of Hypercard, the design and interface sets a high standard.

title: Memory Tools 1.6
description: Memory Tools 1.6 is a stack that contains a number of external commands/functions as well as several cards that show how they can be used effectively. There is a great deal of information in the stack for both programmers and non-programmers alike. All XFCNs and XCMDs are fully documented and illustrated. The external functions include: DisAsm - a full-fledged 68000 disassembler. It even converts trapwords to their appropriate trap names. It contains many options for formatting the output. PeekByte, PeekWord, and PeekLong that take an address and return the value stored at that address. These functions all contain numerous options that enhance their utility. TrapName, TrapWord for converting the name of a trap to its corresponding trap word and vice versa. DecToHex and HexToDec for converting between decimal and hexadecimal values. And more. The stack also includes many demonstrations of how to effectively use the Peek XFCNs in Scripts.

title: MyLibMgr 1.0
description: Organize/manage library references; imports in National Library Format or custom formats, sorts/marks refs, prints, exports, reads RTF text to locate reference sites, etc.; powerful!

title: Navigator Palettes
description: Contains resources for two palettes similiar to the Navigator palette shipped with HyperCard 2.x. One palette is a lengthened version of the Navigator palette, the other is a shortened version.

title: PICT Buttons 3.1.5
description: New features include: * option to automatically delete PICT buttons that were created when you quit the stack. * choice of creating a button to display a PICT file or a PICT resource. * includes tools to add a PICT to another stack as a resource, delete a PICT resource from another stack, or just get a list of PICT resources from another stack. * improved error checking and handling This is a simple stack designed to help novice Hypercard users add color PICTs to their stacks. It automates the process of scripting a button that will display a PICT file in an external window. It also contains additional scripts that can be used to close the external window at the appropriate time (such as going to another card!)

title: Aids
description: A stack containing a pretty morbid looking clock listing the AIDS mortality figures along with the time.

title: Greyline Clock
description: ----------------- Grayline Clock from San Lorenzo Software maps the current day-night boundary throughout the world in real time. The day/night boundary, also called the delimiter, is projected on a modified mercator world map. Every few minutes the map is updated so that it always displays the current delimiter position. The map accurately reflects the current season as well as the time of day and shows how the sun's illumination of the earth varies from summer to winter. This type of map is used by radio amateurs and world band radio listeners to predict worldwide signal propagation.

title: Michigan HyperTextBook 4
description: A HyperText book of cities and places in the state of Michigan. Lists interesting facts and figures.

title: Celestial Wind Carillons.sit
description: ----------------- Designed by the inventor and manufacturer of Celestial Wind Carillons. It has examples of six of his most popular tunings: Greek Temple (a & a short & d pentatonic) Carillon Oriental Temple Carillon Cambridge Carillon Brahm's Lullabye Carillon Actually this is a pretty amazing stack in that it shows off the power of Hypercard and Sound. It really sounds like there's a wind chime in your mac :)

description: A HyperCard stack I designed to teach myself Astrological signs and symbols. The stack works like a set of flashcards. A button will reveal the correct answer. Freeware.

Simple stack allows anyone to take Buckwheat (of the Our Gang Comedy movies (circa 1930's)) to the Barber shop for one of four hairdo's; Mohawk, 'Fro, Wallstreet or sculpt(ure). He looks pretty silly in either. Remember to turn on (and hear) the hair clippers.

title: L.E.D. 2.0
description: This small stack shows you how to create L.E.D. numbers using HyperCard's built-in paint and draw tools.

title: In cerca di fortuna
description: Italian adventure game

title: PC-File Importer
description: The button 'Import PC-File' will import unmodified data files generated with PC-File without having to export the data from PC-File prior to importing into HyperCard. What is PC-File? PC-File is a very popular shareware file manager available for MS-DOS machines. There are many databases available in PC-File format. Fortunately, the data files are column (byte) delimited text files which make for easy importing. I developed the import button inorder to move several commercial landscape plant databases that I have in PC-File format into Hypercard.

title: HyperCard Tenth Anniversary Stack v1.0.1
description: In honor of HyperCard's tenth anniversary in 1997, HyperCard users were invited to submit anniversary greetings and messages concerning how HyperCard has affected their lives. Dozens of HyperCard enthusiasts contributed their thoughts, wishes, and visions. This stack was the result.

title: うらしまさん

title: Sound Effects Stack 8
description: ----------------- 17 Sound resources for HyperCard in a stack.

title: Get Rich Quick
description: Get Rich Quick is PRISIM's first full length adventure game. You are Joe Average our apartment dwelling "hero". You will lead Joe through out the city looking for 100 dollars to pay his rent. Sounds easy but you will find that it's not. You will have to face many things on your quest like man eating dogs, a fat comic book salesman, a mean old lady, and many more. Along the way are puzzles, like how to get on the subway, how to get rid of the dog, what to do with the magic book, and lots of others. Get Rich Quick includes: * Multiple endings. * Optimized for PowerPCs but still works fully with all other Macintoshes. * Animated sequences. * Cartoon appearance. * Multiple logical puzzles. * Lots of items. * Hidden secrets. * Lots of sounds.

title: Music 1.4c
description: This is the update for music 1.3. It is in color! You can play music on a keyboard, or listen to other songs! Wonderful non-MIDI solution to Music playing problems! Check It OUT!!!!

title: Mysterious Drug Store
description: Mysterious Drug Store interactive animated scene

title: Rabbit
description: My first and only animation

title: US Time Zones 2.0
description: Do you call people in other time zones around the country? The map in this stack displays times in different places of the U.S. Select an area code and it'll give you the zone and the time (includes Canada, Alaska and Hawaii)

title: Walt Disney World Money
description: You might want to save or print this. This is a shareware version of Walt Disney World Money. This program will calculate how much it will cost you to have a vacation at Disney World. It has a pulldown menu so that you can choose what hotel that you want to stay at. When you choose a hotel it will put the name of the hotel and how much it will cost per room per night. It also has a button that calculates how much it will cost you to stay at the hotel. It will even let you add in the food cost. Remember that this is only a shareware version. This version does not have all the functions the real version does. Also you can only open this shareware version 15 times. So if you want to keep this program the best way to do so is to get the registered version. To get that you can E-Mail me at: DNoblet@aol.com or you can send $6.50, your name, and address. Mail me at: David Noblet P.O. Box 1381 Londonderry, NH 03053-1381

title: Warp Speed Calculator v1.1 .sit
description: ----------------- Version 1.1 has been updated to calculate either for the original Enterprise (NCC-1701) or the newest Enterprise (NCC-1701-D) using UltraDrive Warp engines. Select the Star Ship; put in the distance (select either Light-years or Parsecs); then click on the desired warp speed. The travel time will then be computed and displayed. Have you ever wondered if Captain Kirk or Captain Picard knows what they are talking about when they quote a certain distance at a certain speed can be covered in a set amount of time????? Example: 'Number One, that's 23 light-years away! Why, that will take 3.5 days at Warp 9!' Well, wonder no more! With the Warp Speed stack you can compute the actual time to cross a given distance at a given speed using the official warp speed factors given in 'The Making of Start Trek' for the old Enterprise (NCC-1701) or the NEW warp speed factors for the new Enterprise (NCC-1701-D). This stack is freeware. Feel free to copy and distribute this stack for non-commercial purposes.

title: HyperCard Japanese System 6 Boot Drive
description: This is a bootable drive of the Japanese variant of MacOS System 6.0.7 loaded with Hypercard 2.4. It is intended to provide a "boot image" for Japanese HyperCard stacks uploaded to the Internet Archive, much in the same way that the HyperCardBootSystem7 item is used (in general) for English-language HyperCard stacks.

title: QuickTimeVers 1.0 XFCN
description: This XFCN reports whether QuickTime is installed and the version number. The version number is particularly significant to developers now that QT 2.0 is available. There are several XFCNs available that perform a similar task. I wrote this one because some developers indicated that they hadn't found a simple XFCN that offered all of the info they needed. Mark

title: Chess Openings
description: 2.2 It is not yet a stand-alone application, hence the need for HC 2.2 I would like to hear from anyone who uses it as to suggestions, bugs, etc.

title: Double-XX Dev Palette
description: This freeware palette is a development utility for users of Heizer Software's Double-XX, and it requires WindowScript to run. It makes it much easier to get to a specific XCMD or XFCN by listing them-Just click on the name of the XCMD to go to its script. Freeware from Jeff Benjamin at StepUp Software.

title: Custom Piano 3.0
description: Piano (and many other) playing program allows point-n-click song creation, import/export of song compositions, and the ability to use any 'snd' resource as an instrument; requires HyperCard/Player 2.2 or 2.3.

title: 50 Filters for WindowScript(D...
description: 'Fifty Filters To Go' contains 50 keyFilter resources for use with WindowScript window fields in the HyperCard environment. WindowScript is available from Heizer Software. A WindowScript key filter is a compiled resource. Key filters can be assigned to a WindowScript window field. Key filters restrict certain characters from being entered into a field or that the data being entered matches a certain format or pattern, such as a telephone number or date. In otherwords, key filters ensure that only certain types of data can be entered into a field. Unlike other key filters, the key filters in 50 Filters To Go don't filter anything. BUT they do call HyperTalk handlers designed by you to filter the data. You can create your own custom keyFilters without having to learn or use a compiler like C or Pascal. You can also test and debug your key filters immediately in HyperCard's run-time, interactive environment. The filters in this stack work exactly like the real thing. Try writing your own Key Filters for WindowScript fields in HyperTalk! --------------------------- Checked with Disinfectant 3.6

title: 玉露
description: Japanese tea making game

title: HAM Radio Logbook
description: After several years as a successful commercial product, the Ham Radio Station Logbook stack is now available as a shareware product ($10). If you are a ham radio operator, you'll find this electronic logbook invaluable to your operation. You can store information on all of your radio contacts with ease for recall at a later date. The Logbook even does some of the work for you, by entering dates and times at the touch of a button. A built-in help system will get you started! If you're a fan of amateur radio, you owe it to yourself to download this stack.

title: HCBalloon 1.2 XCMD
description: ----------------- HCBalloon is a simple XCMD that provides Balloon Help for Hypercard stacks. It can also turn Balloon Help on and off and can check to see if Balloon Help is active. You can also specify the position of the balloon's tip and the place where the tip joins the balloon. HCBalloon is compatible with the Helium system extension. 1.2 is latest version.

title: HCCopyFit 1.0 XFCN
description: HCCopyfit lets you easily copy fit text into a space of a certain size. Starting from a font, size, and style that you provide, HCCopyFit determines how much it needs to shrink the font size in order to make the text fit. Optionally, it can also convert from bold to plain text or from extended to plain text in order to make the copy fit at a given point size. HCCopyfit is freeware, even for commercial use. See the instructions for credits/use information. (c) 1993 Human Computing

title: Henry VI, Part I
description: An annotated arrangement of the play Henry VI, Part I, by William Shakespeare. This stack was authored by Mark Zimmermann, and is part of the Shakespeare Stack Project This stack has been released as Free Software under the GNU General Public License.

title: HPopupMenu XFCN: Hierarchical Popup Menus 2.7.1
description: Hierarchical popup menus v2.7.1

title: Humanitarian.sea
description: orld. You can play for a specified period of time, then evaluate yourself, or you can play until all the problems have been revealed and solved. The challenge of attempting to solve the problems of this changing world will keep you and your friends entertained for hours. ------ Dancing Rabbit Creations produces educational HyperCard programs and games for teachers, students, parents, and learners of all ages. The stacks are affordably priced, and users can try them before they buy - all are released as shareware, or are available in demonstration versions. Dancing Rabbit Creations is the joint (ad)venture of a classroom teacher and a software tester.

title: Hyperbola: A Digital Companion to Gravity's Rainbow
description: This is a HyperCard stack comprising excerpts, notes, and commentaries by an academic and his students. You can add to this structure or change it as you see fit, provided you acknowledge such changes before circulating them to someone else.

title: HyperTree II v1.4++.SEA
description: This self-unstuffing archive contains a data base stack, a separate help stack and a short text document about the data stack plus other goodies. HyperCard version 2.0 stack that stores information and pictures about individuals and family relationships. This allows you to create a professional documents containing significantinformation in an orderly format. Major reports include: (1) a 2 to 12 generation descendancy chart; (2) a 2 to 12 generation pedigree chart; (3) name and date report; (4) report with parent, children and siblings names; (5) a report that lists relatives up to second cousin (6) a 2-12 generation outline format descendancy report (7) four data integrity reports. A companion document is located in a file named HyperTree II v1.4 Doc.SEA. It offers a more detailed description of the features plus samples of screens and reports.

title: HypervoiceDactilo
description: This is rather an in-utilities. It tries to convert spoken language into text.

title: IAC-Resolve
description: This stack features a palette that resembles the Excel 4 toolbar. It's purpose is to allow remote control of Claris Resolve from HyperCard. Works on the same Mac, or simply connect two Macs with a printer cable (use file sharing and program linking on both Macs). Please respond with comments or ideas that can make a more useful version of this stack. Thank you Claris (and Apple)!

title: HyperSpeak.SIT
description: ----------------- HyperSpeak interfaces HyperCard to MacinTalk, letting your HyperCard stack speak arbitrary text. In order to use it, the MacinTalk file must be in your System folder, and the speaker volume should be turned up. You can try it out by entering text in the box below, and then hitting the 'Say it' button; or you can use the message box and invoke the 'speak' command directly. You can also try the buttons above.

title: MachineID XFCN 1.3
description: XFCN which returns the Machine Gestalt ID.

title: The Science of Hanging 1.0
description: This stack is a simple hangman game that can be edited by you! It was designed for science teachers and students. You can easily create your own word lists, solve the puzzle at any time, or choose different lists to play from.

title: TimeCache 1.6 Demo
description: TimeCache is an easy-to-use time tracking application. PandaWare TimeCache is for anybody who charges clients for their time. It lets you keep track of time and billing on projects you work on for different clients. It offers extensive reporting, exporting and printing capabilities.

description: An annotated arrangement of the play Twelfth Night, by William Shakespeare. This stack was authored by Mark Zimmermann, and is part of the Shakespeare Stack Project This stack has been released as Free Software under the GNU General Public License.

title: Video HyperFile 3.0
description: ----------------- ------------------------- This is the Video version of the CD HyperFile. The interface is much the same as CD HyperFile 2.0. This has really helped me with my videos. I recommend it to anybody who has many video. Check it out. I hope that you will enjoy it.

title: Virus Reference 2.1.6
description: Virus Reference is an online database of current Macintosh viruses and trojan horses. You can search for a specific virus name or symptom.

title: Inflate XCMD 2.0
description: ----------------- Use Inflate 2.0 to display balloon help for fields and buttons under System 7.0. Note this external is compatible with System 6.0x however you will not be able to display balloon help unless using System 7. Inflate 2.0 now gives you the ability to display PICT and STR resources within a balloon help. It also allows you to position the point of the balloon in any one of the four corners or directly in the center of the button or field.

title: Supper Time
description: This is a simple, colorful stack to entertain a young child and help develop mouse skills. Your young one can set a make-believe table and the mess cleans itself! No reading required except for Yes and No to quit. This stack has been checked for Mac and HyperCard Viruses with Virex 5.6

title: TreffNet 1.1
description: TreffNet is a simple, Hypercard-based introduction to learning in neural networks. It consists of a tutorial on the simplest of network learning methods called Hebbian learning. The stacks consist of (1) tutorial animations, and (2) a graphical network simulator that can learn to associate two pattern vectors. Based on the first chapter of the classic text by Rumelhart and McClelland (1986), it "brings to life" the basic concepts and mathematics that need to be fully understood before the student tackles more complex systems.

title: Xrules v1.0.4 Demo
description: Xrules adds rule based programming capability to HyperCard or SuperCard. Rule based programming is often used to develop tutorials, diagnostics, help desks, expert systems, etc. You do not need to be a programmer to use Xrules. If you can use HyperTalk, you can use Xrules. This file contains a demo of the Xrules product and a tutorial on rule based programming. The file is compacted with Stuffit 1.5.1 and contains 3 files: ReadMeFirst - This gives an overview of the Xrules product. Xrules Demo - This is a completely functional demo of the actual product. However, it limits the number of rules to 10 while the Xrules product is limited only by memory. Xrules Tutorial - This is a tutorial on rule based programming. We welcome any feedback. Please contact us at integrated lab solutions inc. 1532 Bob Wade Ln. Huntsville, AL 35810 (205) 859-2785 AOL: ILSI AppleLink: ILSI

title: Xrules Demo
description: Provides rule based programming extensions for HyperCard. Demo allows only 10 rules and daemons. Compiler, init, fact, conclusion cards are included with the development stack.

title: YASIX - Yet Another System Infor
description: YASIX - Yet Another System Information XFCN v.2 YASIX provides system information that may or may not be available from other XFCNs. The information includes: machine type, keyboard type, processor type, MMU type, FPU type, physical RAM size, system version, screen size, screen location, screen color, screen bit depth, volume (disk) name, volume size, volume free space, and slot contents. If there is a suggestion for improvement in this or other XFCNs please contact me. I'd be happy to discuss any ideas you might have. Other XFCNs included in this stack are ChooserName, Floating Comment Windoid, and Status Window. Floating Comment Windoid, Status Window and the stack design are from the most excellent Jim Parshall. Make sure you look at the icon animation. Have fun, Clark Brady, Dome Software Corporation

title: HyperStage
description: This stack is an interactive primer in stagecraft. It teaches the parts of a proscenium stage, the basics of stage direction, and allows the user to create set designs that can be printed out. The stack was designed for middle school students who volunteered to work on the stage crew of the school play.

title: AppleEventTalk 1.2.7; New Fea...
description: HCard is HyperCard (2.x), well then again, you could use supercard.... This is a new version of the appleEventTalk stack. It has the folowing changes: Talking; This stack talks. It says what is sent and recieved if you want it to. It does not if you do not want it to. AppleEventTalk is a way of 'talking' to other people over a network. It sends apple Events to do this. (That's were the name is from) You can talk as long as you have Hypercard and this stack. It has things to paste in the home stack script. Then you can be alerted when someone asks to talk to you. Have fun. No charge for it.

title: Ballpark Hardball Figures
description: Just a little stack for HardBall II users in mind. I have the complete team rosters for 1994 so if you print it out and use the team editor in HardBall, you will have the real teams with the real names. Also good for people who like baseball.

title: Bo's Bits 1.1
description: Can you identify Bo Diddley tracks from brief sound extracts? Also contains a short bio. (This version corrects the "Dearest Darling" error.)

title: CD Cache
description: CD Cache is a HyperCard standalone application which can run on any Macintosh, though a color monitor is recommended. CD Cache is a tool for organizing your CD ROM collection. Using HyperCard's easy data entry and retrieving capabilities, you have several options to organize and print your CD ROM collection.

title: Dance with Tiny
description: Tiny, a colorized button, demonstrates notated phrases so that you may easily read the box step, waltz, and Macarena. The Labanotated version of the Macarena is based on Lucy Venable's Motif Writing description through measure 12 and the final measures offer a variation. (Located at AOL's Macintosh Music & Sound Forum, the music's source is a sound file made by Shtetl. Neither its uploader nor I know who the original composer is. Apparantly, there were no known shareware fees or disclaimers posted.) This stack is notationware. If you like it, please consider sending a contribution to the Dance Notation Bureau in New York City, or send email to let me know if Tiny helped you to learn Labanotation. -------- Checked for Mac and HyperCard viruses with Virex 5.6

title: Engines
description: A great demonstration of Gasoline, Diesel and Steam engines with animated examples of them running and discussions of the principles involved.

title: AlertBox XCMD 1.2
description: Yet another stale dialog box external? No way. I was always depressed by HyperCard's inability to display dialog boxes with pizazz. The only thing close to a dialog box was the 'answer' command, which proves useful in all ways but one: you can do almost nothing with it. The only thing you can do is specify text and button names. So I wrote this simple external. It displays a custom ALRT resource, which is dismissed when the user clicks on a button. You can't do anything else with the dialog, though. I wanted this external to be simple. It's ideal for displaying an "About..." message, and you don't need complex dialog items for that.

title: Armchair Shrink 2.0 Update

Take the test & gain some insight into your own psyche. A fine party game, too; all take it simultaneously using notepads, then everyone's individual results are viewed in light of the analyses. What's New in 2.0: More reliable error traps; new b&w graphics for display on older machines, i.e., Classics; Help File. -- Checked with Virex 5.1

title: Better Sound Stack.sit

title: BetterBoard 1.3

It uses a series of built-in images for quick development of 'rough drafts' and also allows the user to import bitmap images for a more 'polished' storyboard.

title: Capital Idea
description: This single-purpose HyperCard stack allows you to import a text file, change the letters to all lower case or all upper case, or to reverse the case of all letters in a file. The altered text can then be written to a new text file, and the user can transfer directly from HyperCard to his word processor of choice. Capital Idea is useful for transposing files in which a person has typed IN ALL CAPITAL LETTERS, for turning a file into all caps, and for certain types of telecomm file transfers, in which the case of the text becomes transposed in transmission. The author asks a $5 shareware fee to encourage further development, and just because he's a really nice guy and you'd want to help him out anyway.

title: casa
description: una casa interattiva

title: Cat
description: This new version fixes a bug that did not allow commas in the file names. In addition, the updated version of Roger Brown's ListDialog XFCN has been installed. What?! Another floppy disk cataloguer? True; however, here are some reasons this stack is worth a look: 1. It's free. 2. It allows for completely HANDS OFF cataloguing of multiple disks. (You only need to type if you want to add comments) 3. The size of each disk, the memory used, the memory left, and the list of files on the disk are automatically read and loaded. 4. It generates disk labels on standard Avery 5196 and even lets you print on a partially used page of labels. 5. It has nice graphics and sound. 6. It has on-line help. 7. It automatically sorts your disks (alphabetically) and creates and index. A few of the resources in the stack (XCMD) are shareware- if you intend to use them for your own projects please see the READ ME file for information. The stack itself is free. I checked it for virii with DI 2.9

title: Cat Box 1.0

It's main part is a diary that allows you to record such important dates as when you planted, harvested, sprayed for insects, etc. It also provides a section for making records of the plants themselves such as watering needs, light requirements, location in the garden, or whatever you feel is useful to you. It also provides some basic information about soil and watering, and a place to make notes about pests in your garden. Give it a try. Any comments would be appreciated to make future versions more useful.

title: ADBLights XCMD
description: ----------------- This totally cool XCMD turns on and off those three green lights in the upper-right corner of your extended ADB keyboard! Make patterns, make the beach-ball more exciting. Doen't affect other keyboards. Get it today!

title: Addition Addendum
description: Notes on Addition Addendum: This is one of the comercially distrubuted educational products of Sure Death Software. We have now made it available for the general public. It is an addition and subtraction stack for children ages 5 through 12 (even teenagers could use some practice very once in a while) It operates fairly simply, to start the game, click on the start game button on the first card, then you will be given instructiones which read somewhat as follows: 'Figure out the answers to these equations, type them in, then click on the answer button.' When the child has answered 10, 20, or 50 equations correctly (number depends on difficulty level) the car that has been racing little by little across the road shall reach its finish line. We hope your child loves this program!!! Write JStancato with questions, comments, and suggestions.

title: Bruchrechnen

title: Caesar, de Bello Gallico vi.1...

If you know Latin, you'll like this novel way to read Caesar. Information on ordering the full version is included.

title: capIt XFCN.sit
description: ----------------- 'capIt' is an XFCN for HyperCard or SuperCard that converts the first character or all characters in a word to upper- or lowercase. The word can be of any length, and in the form of any valid HyperTalk or SuperTalk container. Crafted with CompileIt! 1.5. This stack includes resources and scripts that will install capIt in another stack. A shorter version (by about 30K) of this stack without the installation resources and scripts has also been uploaded to AOL.

title: Cartographer
description: Cartographer is an XFCN for HyperCard that draws a grid of cicns VERY fast and easily. This XFCN is perfect for RPGs. Until now color HyperCard RPGs were either black and white or color slow and choppy. I saw some good color HyperCard RPGs but got tired of watching that wave of cicns each time the map was drawn. Well no more wave because cartographer draws ANY size grid with ANY size cicns in just 2 - 3 ticks all at once!!! Best of all if you've been looking for something like this but don't want to spend big bucks then here it is... only $5 to register and gain the ability to use in your own stacks. This XFCN is for anybody who is looking to make a good, smooth, and fast RPG in HyperCard. Staff Notes: - Nice effort for a grid map of ICON, CICN tiles ... many creative possibilities. - All files are checked for known viruses before being made available for downloading

title: Compression XFCNs
description: ----------------- This stack contains shareware XFCNs that compress data using the Lempel-Ziv-Welch algorithm. One pair of XFCNs compresses and uncompresses files (including the resource fork). Another pair deals with HyperTalk strings. You might use these XFCNs if you wanted to manipulate compressed data under the control of a script.

title: CompressPictFile XCMD 1.3
description: CompressPictFile compresses a PICT file using QuickTime™ JPEG.

title: CopyFile
description: copyfile sourcefile, destinationfile, CopyOverOldFile, options sourcefile - optional input file path - dialog will ask if not present destinationfile - optional output file path - dialog will ask if not present CopyOverOldFile - yes, no, or cancel - default is no • yes writes over old file • no brings up dialog to ask when destination exists • cancel aborts if destination exists options - turn invisible and custom icon bits on or off • makeVisible turns file visible • makeInvisible turns file invisible • hasCustomIcon sets custom icon bit • noCustomIcon clears custom icon bit Special Features: • Uses dynamic memory for buffer and tries to copy files in one pass. • Uses Multifinder temporary memory if available. • Copies icon color. • Saves icon position when overwriting destination. • Can prompt for input and output files.

title: CursorState 1.0
description: CursorState is an XCMD that will hide, show, or obscure the cursor. The cursor is left in this state until another call is made to CursorState or HyperCard updates the screen.

title: doTitles & curlyQuotes XFCNs
description: doTitles & curlyQuotes XFCNs Sunday, June 21, 1998 -------------------------------------------------------------------- doTitles XFCN (v. 1.0) and curlyQuotes XFCN (v. 1.0) -------------------------------------------------------------------- Size, expanded: 65K. Public domain XFCNs. -------------------------------------------------------------------- doTitles() XFCN id #31611 v. 1.0 This external function capitalizes words in titles. Unlike other functions and scripts I've seen, it doesn't just capitalize the first letter of each word indiscriminately. Instead, it capitalizes the first and last words of the title and all nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, adverbs, and subordinate conjunctions; it does not capitalize articles, coordinate conjunctions, or prepositions, regardless of length, unless they are the first or last words of the title. If the title contains a subtitle preceded by a colon, it capitalizes the first word of the subtitle too. If the title contains only capital letters or has otherwise been capitalized improperly, it recapitalizes according to the criteria above. If there are straight up-and-down quotation marks around the title (') or odd substitutions for quotation marks (`` or ''), it converts them to curved, left- and right-handed marks. It also converts single quotation marks and apostrophes. It's smart enough to recognize some contractions ('til, 'twas, and so forth), but not all possible contractions. It capitalizes abbreviations if they contain periods (U.S.A. or Ph.D.), but not if they don't (ACLU or SJ). (That's because it doesn't know whether ACLU is a word or an abbreviation.) It converts double hyphens (--) into real em dashes, and hyphens between dates and numbers in series (1901-76) into real en dashes. Finally, it converts double or multiple spaces into single spaces. curlyQuotes() XFCN id #19501 v. 1.0 This function also converts quotation marks and apostrophes to the curved variety, but it does nothing else. ------------------------------------------------------------------- Syntax: put doTitles(container) into container put curlyQuotes(container) into container -where container is any HyperCard container, such as a field or variable. ------------------------------------------------------------------- doTitles and curlyQuotes come installed in a demonstration stack. To install these external functions in another stack, you must use a resource-copying utility, such as ResCopy, ResEdit, or ResMover. -------------------------------------------------------------------

description: ----------------- THIS IS THE LATEST RELEASE OF THE FANTASY BASEBALL MANAGER AS OF 4-23-91. KEEPS TRACK OF ALL ROTISSERIE LEAGUE STYLE FUNCTIONS AND STATS. PRINTS REPORTS OF LEAGUE STANDINGS. YOU CAN IMPORT SPORTS STATS OFF A WIRE SERVICE. ALL BUGS IN PREVIOUS RELEASES ARE NOW FIXED. RUN YOUR LEAGUE WITH HALF THE COSTS OF FANTASY STAT SERVICES!

Have fun and remember practice makes perfect... So don't get addicted to this stack ('The angelic side of the conscience'). -Chris *************************************************** Checked for viruses with CP Anti-Virus 3.0 ***************************************************

title: House of Multiplication Horro...
description: The House of Multiplication Horror AlphaDog SoftWare by Glen L. Bledsoe Just in time for Halloween! This stack is a big improvement over flashcards -- including my previous 'Flashy Flashcards.' It can used by an individual, but was designed especially for use by a classroom. If the answer is correct a random visual effect will take place and a creepy sound will be heard. If the answer is wrong, the correct answer will be shown in the message box. If a student takes too long to answer the fact, even if his or her answer is correct, they will not get credit for having mastered the fact, but will receive a reward for having gotten the right answer. This stack discards the facts that students already know and proceeds to drill them in groups of four only on the facts that they have shown that they do not know or do not know quickly. After the student demonstrates that he or she knows a given fact well (by getting it correct 4 times while being shuffled in with 3 other facts), that fact is discarded and is replaced by another fact that the student had trouble with previously until all 91 multiplication facts are mastered. Research indicates that this is a very effective way to use flashcards. This stack will not forget where a student left off in his or her practice from day to day. They can come back at any time and pick up where they left off. This stack works best having the PostCrypt Font in your System Folder! It is available elsewhere on AOL. This is Freeware! Share is with anyone who can use it.

title: HYPERCARD CROSS HAIRS
description: Cross hairs is a stack which demonstrates some easy button animation and what is possible with hypercard and a little hypertalk experience. This stack has a set of of cross hairs which pinpoint the mouse location on the card. The movement of the cross hairs is very smooth and very quick. This stack is shareware. It is limited to three uses unless you send me the $5.00 shareware fee. When i receive the shareware fee i will e mail you the password which will allow you to use the stack as much as you want as well as see the script. The cross hairs can be incorporated into your own for some neat efeects. Please free to use these scipts in your own stacks.

title: Schedule Master - business
description: f products. Schedule Master allows the user to generate a weekly schedule, manipulate it , and even print it. It calculates your employees' hours by employee, by day, and total hours scheduled. It also keeps a record of all past schedules and allows you to recall any past schedule. Keyware, $10.00

title: SpecChek Spell Checking Demo
description: Enclosed in this archive is the SpecChek HyperCard Spell Checking System Demo from Spectral Design. This allows you to see how you can use HyperCard and SpecChek to spell check text in documents by comparing it with a list of words (enclosed is a 46,000 word dictionary plus a user dictionary). If you like this system you can liscense it for $10 (or $5 if you prefer the software to be e-mailed to you) and you will get detailed instructions on how to implement the SpecChek system into your own Hypercard stacks. Registered users will also get a free upgrade to the SpecChek XCMD due out soon which will dramatically increase ease of use for the programmer and the speed of spell checking for the user. You will also get a free version of SpectreWrite Pro v2.0 which incorporates the SpecChek system. I hope you'll find this useful. -Justin Higgins Spectral Design Checked for Mac & HC viruses

It requires Hypercard version 2.+ and preferably a color capable Macintosh since it contains some colored photomicrographs of erythrocytes.
