Check out the 'WorkShop' area where can you work with various table properties and even create your own table! This demo gives you 30 days - beginning when you want! - to try out its various features. There is no preset expiration date; it will expire 30 days after you begin using it. Lee Buck, creator of WindowScriptx says: '. . .with ListTable developers can break through the limitations of one-card-at-a-time viewing and extend the power of HyperCard.' 'ListTable is the best solution we've seen for dealing with tabular data in HyperCard.' -- Brian Molyneau, Heizer Software. Call (800) 972-2483 or Fax (212) 663-4503 if you need more information or would like to own your own copy. ListTable comes with a 100 page manual including an interactive tutorial and dozens of examples and tips. With ListTable you can: * Edit ListTable's Custom 'LTBL' resource Quickly and Easily with the LT Attributes Editor * Choose from 85 Programmable Attributes including Styles, Colors, Alignments, Fonts, and much much more * Use Custom Column Headings and Row Numbering * Resize Columns by Clicking and Dragging or from Your Scripts * Search by Columns, Selected Cells, or Entire Table * Sort by Text, Number, Decimal, Date, or Time * Print any Table with Headers, Footers and Page Numbering * Delete, Insert and Append Columns and Rows * Display Text greater than 32K * Set Column Alignments * Have hidden columns * Work with Contiguous and Non-contiguous Selections * Access Individual Cells, Rows or Columns from any Script * Choose document or palette layer windows with any WDEF * Use Delimiters of Your Choice for Fields and Records * Set Columns to any Style, or Combination of Styles * Create powerful and attractive database front-ends * Work with Multiple Monitors using global or local coordinates * Control the Color of any part of any window * Customize how ListTable Communicates with Your Stacks * Use a Sidebar WDEF palette (Comes free with ListTable!) * Create tables and lists with Heizer Software's 'Double-XX' * MouseDown/MouseUp Messages * Scrollbar Messages for Dynamically Inserting/Deleting Data * 30 Day Money Back Guarantee * ListTable sales for $149! * To Order Call Toll-Free: (800) 972-2483, or (212) 865-3596; Fax (212) 663-4503

title: Scientific Notation XFCN 1.0....
description: Scientific Notation XFCN 1.0.... Sunday, June 21, 1998 4). Numbers are converted into scientific number notation and vice versa. Significant figures (including trailing zeroes) are preserved. It's free. This version fixes a bug introduced when adding treatment for significant trailing zeroes.

title: The Winter's Tale
description: An annotated arrangement of the play The Winter's Tale, by William Shakespeare. This stack was authored by Mark Zimmermann, and is part of the Shakespeare Stack Project This stack has been released as Free Software under the GNU General Public License.

title: The MagicFields 1.0
description: The MagicFields 1.0 is an easy-to-use tool to make your stack look nicer, adding fields that can appears or disappears wherever you like. It is not a XCMD or a XFNC but a field that you can easy modify to fit it into your stacks. The MagicFields allows you to zoom in or out the font of your field, giving a command that you can handle by scripts.

title: GeoDraw USA
description: GeoDraw USA is shareware. GeoDraw USA helps geography students learn about the 50 states. They are given a blank map and instructions for the state of their choice. The finished product can be quite detailed. Look for GeoDraw Latin America and GeoDraw USA for HyperStudio.

title: Forbidden Secrets from Beyond HyperCard
description: This stack was designed, created, and scripted by Rand Miller and Robyn Miller, of Cyan, (the Manhole and Cosmic Osmo guys) for MacUser Magazine and you (the happy user).

title: EucaQuiz v3.8
description: Help and Instructions EucaQuiz has two functions. The first is to provide budding botany and dendrology students with a detailed reference of the common eucalypt species found in Victoria. All reference material has been sourced from "Eucalypts of South Eastern Victoria" by Leon Costermans (See the references page for full details). The second function of Eucaquiz is to encourage learning of the material found within the book "Eucalypts of South Eastern Victoria" as well as to reinforce your field observations. Eucaquiz is fun, challenging and competitive. Not only does the quiz keep a record of your performance, it also records the time you took to complete the quiz! Species reference instructions The species index lists all the species found in EucaQuiz. Simply click a species to go to it. Once there you are able to view detailed information for that species, including drawings of buds, leaves and location of natural stands. Alternatively you can browse through the different species by using the 'next' and 'previous' buttons. Quiz instructions The quiz is really simple to use. Just click on the 'Quiz' button to start the quiz. You will be asked to enter your full name and the type of quiz. When all the questions have been answered the quiz will stop automatically and take you to the score board. If you got any questions wrong then you are able to get a printout of the questions you got wrong, but not the suggested answers. It's up to you to learn the correct answers. Whilst undertaking the quiz if you get a question wrong the correct answer is highlighted temporarily before the quiz moves on to the next question. Good luck and if you have and suggestions or bugs to report please send me a note via email at j.reed@forestry.unimelb.edu.au

title: Port~Master.SIT
description: ----------------- Documentation and installer for cdev that allows easy modification of the serial port PRAM settings.

title: Portraits of Filipinos Part 3
description: Third art stack containing quite a few portraits of various friends, relatives, or people I've seen. Includes some bonus colour cards... Made w/various Macs, Fractal Design Painter or Sketcher, & a Wacom ArtZ ADB tablet. For more, do a Filesearch & enter search phrase Rome Dome. Enjoy! Rome Dome ----------------- Staff Note: Sketch pad of decent art, some color. Checked for Viruses with Disinfectant & MerryXmas Vaccine

title: The Complete Gen. Family Tree 2.0 Demo
description: A family tree organizer.

title: Nirvana Music Lyrics
description: This HyperCard stack has a complete discography as well as lyrics to Nirvana songs. It also has histories etc...

title: Terra Nova
description: A simple military-economics game between the Alfamaxos and the Zediminos on the world of Nova Terra.

title: Johnさん
description: Picture book about John (creator of Disinfectant)

title: Maine HyperTextBook 4
description: A HyperText book of cities and places in the state of Maine. Lists interesting facts and figures.

title: Making Marks 1.0a6
description: Making Marks is a stack that contains the 'Marks' palette which is a management utility for HyperCard's mark card feature. The palette provides buttons that execute the various commands associated with marking, navigating by and counting marked cards. Includes an installer which places the appropriate scripts to support the palette, or you can put the stack in use. The stack's cantModify property is set to true to prevent it from growing. This stack and its palette are free, but cannot be distributed commercially without my permission.

title: LinksBase 1.1
description: A HyperCard database for web directories. LinksBase allows you to maintain a database of links and HTML code templates, and build specialized pages out of them automatically. It is really only meant for building pages that are, essentially, lists of links, like Yahoo! for example (although it would be utterly innapropriate for Yahoo! itself): it doesn't have much to offer someone posting poetry to the Web. I designed LinksBase mostly for my own benefit, and the cheesy interface should be a dead giveaway that I wasn't thinking too hard about commercial potential. This stack will be of most use to someone who is reasonably comfortable with both HTML and HyperTalk: most people will not want to use the templates I have provided, and in fact, to get the most out of it, you may want to add a few fields, which will necessitate adding some new code. The stack comprises four main parts: the Detail cards, which is where you keep information on individual links, the Categories cards, which is where you set up a page-level and subcategory-level hierarchy, the HTML Code cards, where you insert chunks of HTML code to be used as templates, and the Build card, where you make it all happen.

title: MacHai! English/Japanese.SIT
description: ----------------- Learn Katakana & Hiragana along with stroke order. Also use flash cards with English to Japanese dictionary. Includes verbal pronunciation from English to Japanese using Macintalk. Extensive use of XCMD's with pop-up menus, etc.

title: Macintosh Consultants
description: ----------------- A very well designed stack listing Macintosh Consultants. If you are in need of someone to assist in hardware or software aquisition/training or need something custom prorammed look here. A valuable resource for all those who need to know.

title: MonthLong XFCN.sit
description: ----------------- MonthLong XFCN returns the number of days in a given month. You may already be using a HyperTalk or SuperTalk script for that; however this XFCN is faster and a little more versatile. For example, if you pass no parameters, it returns the number of days in the current month. If you pass the month only, it assumes the current year. Naturally, you can also supply the year. Best of all, you can pass the month as either a name (long or abbreviated) or an integer of any size. Non-commercial use of this XFCN is free. =George=

title: MouseUp 1.5
description: MouseUp is a Hypercard stack to manage data for a transgenic mouse colony. Its particular purpose is to generate an index file of the individual mice in the colony. Each mouse has an index card listing the mouse's ID, sex, birthdate, genotype, parents, and offspring. Also there is a field for entering any text as notes. Other features include ability to search the database for any entry in any field, and automatic updating of the offspring field of parent mice when new information is entered. But the coolest thing is that the mouse database is a hypertext family tree. In other words, there is a link from any mouse's card to the cards of its parents and its offspring. Just point and click the mother listed on a mouse's card (for example) and jump immediately to the card of the mother mouse. Then click the mouse you just came from, listed under offspring, and go back.

title: MoveCursor.sit
description: This is one I've been looking for for a while. An XCMD which allows you to position the cursor instantly with using drag commands. Simply call moveCursor and the coordinates you want and the cursor instantly appears at the loc specified. I wanted this so I could have the control pallette of Reports 2 appear where I wanted. KWs: mouse,cursor,position,coodinates,Reports,XCMD

title: MultiScroll XCMD 1.0
description: Great external from Oscar Hills enables smooth multiple scrolling fields in HyperCard.

title: Music
description: Birds sitting on a branch sing a selction of songs. Programmed by 8th grade students at Powell Middle School, Littleton, CO.

title: Musical Chairs
description: Musical Chairs Demo is a nifty little stack, or database, for your music. You can list them by title, artist, or tracks & notes. I am only asking $1.00 for shareware, even though the stack says $5.00. PLEASE READ THE INCLUDED DOCUMENTATION. Thanx, Kleinow338

title: Norbik Nudley (aka Chuck and Leo's Really Nifty Adventure)
description: Norbik Nudley is a comedy adventure game by Nick Longrich starring Chuck and Leo. The humor is in the vein of Sam and Max.

title: NullSet.sit 1.1
description: Nullset 1.1 is an update that fixes some bugs in the earlier version. If you are using an earlier version of Nullset you should download this one. The Nullset stack contains 3 externals written in CompileIt! The CompileIt! code is included for your perusal and edification. Hypercard does not like the lowly null, and externals are required for those few who need to deal with this character. OneNull is an xcmd that allows you to read or write a null from or to a file. CountNulls() is an xfcn that tells you how many nulls are in a designated file. NoNulls is an xcmd that allows you to either replace all nulls in a file with a replacement string of your choice, or replace all occurrences of a string with nulls. This file has been checked with Disinfectant 3.0.

title: Tab Player 1.1
description: I have searched for a program that plays guitar-tab's, but i haven't found any. So I decided to make my own. And NWS Tab Player, is the result! Tab Player 1.1, isn't so complicated (it just took me a few ours to made), but it will be better in the future.

title: Investment Manager 2.60
description: Recognizes stocks,bonds,mortgages,mutual funds,CDs, more. Track/record price changes,dividends/interest, gains, etc. Data export-enabled.

title: VCR
description: This stack can store information about all of your movies, which is great for those of you who have a hard time remembering what you have and what their about.

title: Auto
description: ----------------- This stack is for those do-it-your-self auto maintenance buffs who enjoy keeping track of the service work they do on their cars.

title: Buuud
description: It's commencement day at St. Olaf and somebody has stole all the diplomas. Kind of a goofy adventure game that has minimal graphics and sounds.

title: GetIdleTime XFCN
description: This XFCN accesses the Gestalt selector installed by the IdleTime INIT and returns the time, in ticks since the last user event. Source included! Enjoy! Brian Blood Maverick Enterprises

title: Merry Christmas Virus Killer
description: Tool created to help combat the MerryXMAS HyperCard virus.

title: HyperMemory 1.0.1
description: HyperMemory is a funny game that I have written more than 2 years and a half ago. I was 16 1/2 years old. This stack show how to write a 'Memory' game under HyperCard 2.x. The stack is unlocked, so you show all the code. The only important thing that be aware is that the 'Do not interrupt' button in 'Protect Stack' MUST be checked, because the card mixing algorithm may trash the stack if it is interrupted. This stack is public domain, and may be distributed freely everywhere. AOL, Compu and BBS users, please upload it into the libraries if it is not in.

title: MergeStacks
description: ----------------- A button that will merge any two stacks together. You will need to modify the button script to name the fields from the second stack that you want transfered to the combined stack.

title: Oregon HyperTextBook 4
description: A HyperText book of cities and places in the state of Oregon. Lists interesting facts and figures.

title: Password Implementation
description: Password hack stack is an implementation of the Rinaldi XFCN in an easy format for understanding Hypertalk. The code is fully commmented. No guessing as to how anything works. The XFCN returns a 6 digit number as the result of typing in a word. You can then assign any Hypercard or system commands after you test, boolean, or calculate a value based on that number. The Rinaldi XFCN is excellent in that as you type in the number, the actual digits are invisible. What an observer sees is just the typical bullets on screen as you type. This stack is FREEWARE. Use, dissect, hack at will. Not real security, but good for a classroom, or public access stacks.

title: PrintJobInfo 1.1
description: An XFCN that puts up a modified dialog similar to that of PrintJobInfo (gets the number of copies, start and end page, etc.). Returns these values to HyperCard so you can handle these appropriately.

title: PurgeClicks (Event Queue) XFCN
description: Removes excess mouseUps and mouseDowns from the event queue. Used to prevent accidental clicking on a destination card when the user impatiently clicks the mouse while the poor stack tries to keep up. Most useful in SuperCard, as this seems more succeptable to this problem than HyperCard. $5 shareware

title: QuickTime Text Tracks 1.0
description: QuickTime™ Text Tracks is an Apple HyperCard™ based utility for easily creating text tracks for your QuickTime™ movies. Text tracks may be used for movie captions, HREFTracks, URLs, or movie Chapter popup menus.

title: Réflexes
description: Try to react as soon as possible !

title: Resume Enhancer
description: ----------------- Enhance that one page resume with a stack designed to present the best of you. This public domain stack template was written with simplicity and style, feel free to customize and adapt.

title: General Purpose Note Stack
description: ----------------- General Purpose Note Stack converted from HC 1.0

title: DriveSpinning XFCN
description: A HyperCard XFCN that lets you know if your drive is currently spinning or not.

title: Development Tools.sit
description: This stack was written as an aid to HyperTalk programmers. It produces: -> Complete documentation of a card or a stack -> Lists of global variables in a stack -> Sorted global variable cross-reference list Both global listings and cross-references require the use of an external function to find the word global in a script. The cross references further require a sort function. Both of these are included in this stack so the stack will work correctly without modification. You may want to copy these XCMDs to your home stack or Hypercard to make things run smoother.

title: Astrology Treasure Hunt Demo
description: ASTROLOGY, PLANETS, SIGNS, HOROSCOPE, ZODIAC,TREASURE HUNT GAME, ASTEROIDS, FREEWARE DEMO, MAC ______________________ When is the Leo time to play, the Saturn time to work, the Venus time to love? Just as the Sun rises and sets every day, so do all of the other planets (Sun, Moon through Pluto, and asteroids Ceres, Pallas, Vesta, and Juno) have a 5-10 minute period when they are on the angles (rising, setting, culminating and at the nadir). This program generates a listing of when the planets are on the angles and when the angles change sign FOR YOUR LOCATION AND TIME. Includes interpretation for each planet and sign. I call the first, lowest level of significance, the 'forced' level. For example, if you see that Mars is on the Ascendant, you should look around for sharp or metal objects. You would know what you wanted to find (things, events, and people 'ruled' by Mars), and start looking for them. The next level I call the 'awareness' level. Keeping with the Mars example, you might become aware that you have been sitting on wrought iron chairs for an hour. The last, third, and highest level is the 'event' level. You might see on TV one or two police with all of their Mars equipment (gun, handcuffs, baton, etc.). This demo version has all the features of the commercial version except printing is limited (complete listing displayed on screen). The commercial version may be ordered from the author below for $25. The features new to 'Astrology Treasure Hunt 2.0c' are: => Asteroids Ceres, Pallas, Juno, Vesta and planet Chiron have been added. These symbolize many feminine traits not covered by Moon and Venus: Sexuality leading to spiritual awakening; nuturing all life; the rules necessary for one-to-one relationships; wisdom needed for people to live together cooperatively => Much expanded interpretation of all planets and signs => True type 'Glyphs' font print beautifully -- thanks to Time Cycles Research cooperation in my font development. => Retrogrades shown by 'R' Gerald Baron M.S Professional Astrologer since 1972 POB 426501 San Francisco, CA 94142-6501 (415) 751-4661 voice/eve & weekend Internet: GeraldCA@aol.com 'The years teach things the days never knew.' Checked for Mac and HyperCard viruses with virex 5.6

title: Barr LD Database Reader
description: Read in the flat file version of the Barr Digital Laser Disc Catalog available via anonymous ftp from cs.yale.edu as /pub/rec.video/BarrLDDB/lib.dat. The database contains records for ALL currently available NTSC laser discs with information on letterboxing, digital stereo, close captioning, availability, etc.

title: Apple //e File Decoder 1.1
description: This program will let you open files created on an Apple II series microcomputer. It will let you open text files, BSAVEd text, Lo-Res graphics, and Hi-Res graphics, and Applesoft program files.

title: Plato Learning Math 1st.-5th
description: PLATO LEARNING MATH: Children Math From 1st Grade to 5th GradeThey Will Learn Math, Improve There Grades Learn As Much As You Want. You Will Learn Addition, Subtraction, Greater Or Less, Divison 1, Multiplication Tables, Multiplication 1, Divison 5, Multiplication 5,Handle Money, Basic Problem Solving. Age 5 to 11years Note: Has fun sounds like Harspsicord,chopper,you said it goofup from Hypercard ToolBox.But are not needed to play.(R) (STAFF NOTE: The user interface may be too confusing for children to use unassisted.)

title: PopModel 1.0
description: PopModel Version 1.0 Freeware PopModel is a HyperCard stack that uses a simple population projection model to predict future populations on a country by country basis. The stack contains information about the demographic, environmental and economic conditions in 218 countries, and you can alter the data to see the effect that changes in the environmental and economic indicators have on the population projections. You can also export the data for further analysis. The stack is DESIGNED for classroom (or home teaching) use with extensive help, a glossary of terms, and detailed instructions for several projects suitable for either individual enrichment or group assignments. The author will actively support classroom teachers who want to make use of this software. This stack is based on the idea that real learning comes not from the accumulation of disconnected facts, but instead is the natural outcome of using real world tools to accomplish meaningful work. Scanned with Sam 3.0

title: Word Bingo 1.5
description: Have you got some terms you want your students to review in a non-boring way. Try playing a Bingo game with your terms instead of definition sheets. WORDBINGO 1.5 is the latest version of Word Bingo. It will create as many full sheet bingo cards as you want, each with the 24-25 terms you gave in a different random order. No two cards alike. New to 1.5, larger font and choose to have a 'FREE' space or not. WORDBINGO 1.5 will keep up to 28 word lists of terms and definitions so you can have a list for each subject and unit. WORDBINGO 1.5 will work with any printer, dot or laser. It is a great educational game for only $6.00 Shareware fee. It fits any subject/grade level. Let me know how you like it. -PWSearcey

title: MiniText 1.0.1
description: Open any text file using this handy hypercard word processor.

title: HCtoWWW 1.02
description: HCtoWWW is an HyperCard to HTML Translator. It allows to translate all text contained in a stack to HTML page(s) with many options including styles, HTML hypertext links, URL detection, automatic Titles, T.O.C., image tags and more.

title: Construction Costs
description: Construction_Costs allows the user to record each payment of a construction project (new house, new bedroom, etc.), and then categorize the payments into a summary. Invaluable record for future tax purposes. --------------- Staff Note: Building Budget from Owner's Perspective, Dialog entry control of items and costs which are summarized to one of 26(max) main categories which can be renamed. Compares budget to posted costs. Scanned for Viruses with Disinfectant and Merry Xmas Vaccine.

title: Developers Time Clock
description: ----------------- Developers Time Clock is a stack and a self-installing script. This script is auto-installed in the stack script of the stack you are developing. The Time Clock stack contains a log for each stack you have installed it on. When you open and close your stack, the date, starting time, ending time, and elapsed time are automatically posted to the appropriate log. Now you will always know how many hours to date you have spent developing a stack. (pretty depressing, huh?) Well, maybe you are doing it on a consulting basis in which case it should help you make bucks. THIS IS FREEWARE This stack auto-unstuffs.

title: Devinombre
description: Let the Mac try to find the number you are thinking of

title: Drawing Isometric!
description: An excellent resource for tech or shop teachers and students. A simple stack with instructions on drawing isometric sketches. Please read the Read me file first. I've included some isometic sketches of mine in PICT format. Enjoy and send me lots of email! Crusher170

title: Investing - Options Analysis
description: ----------------- This stack is used to compute the intrinsic value for a call or put option. Very easy to use. If you have a need for custom developed stacks, templates, or databases, drop me a line.

title: Maclectic BBS Tour
description: ----------------- This is a stack that gives you a tour of Maclectic BBS in Whittier, CA. The stack is full of sounds and special effects. It describes all parts of this Second Sight BBS. It also offers a discount for downloading privileges in the stack. Maclectic BBS offers over 1600 files for downloading, over 22,000 messages in 2 years and a special Clip Art service for those involved in Desk Top Publishing.

title: Magic and Mindreading
description: zard is an amazing mind reading stack. It WILL fool you, and it is ALWAYS right! With some thought, maybe you can figure out the secret?

title: HyperTrax Converter.SIT
description: ----------------- What is this thing? This is a utility stack which converts most HyperTrax,SoundWave, SoundCap and other digital sound files that are a.k.a. 'FSSD',into 'Snd' resources, which can be used for HyperCard stacks, Mac2 beeps or anywhere else you can call up the resource. It is input sensitive, but it is not output sensitive...so be careful! This is a resource editor, so handle it like a gun. I've seen grown men cry because they had thought it would be funny to put the theme to the 'Munsters' into their only copy of PageMaker. It is important that you keep in mind that digital sounds are RAM eaters. They do not get along well with other RAM dwellers such as talking mooses (meese?), screen savers, RAM cache's, trick clocks, etc...See what works with your system. Operation: The purpose of this program is to convert a sound file into a resource, and then install it on to a stack or application of your choice. Clicking within either the Source Sound icons or on the Source Path field, will prompt you to open a sound file. After selecting your sound, the Source Path will indicate the path to your selection. Target selection works the same way, only it will let you select ANYTHING you want to send the sound to.

title: TCP NewsTalk 1.1
description: An example stack which displays HyperCard TCP capabilities. It connects up to whichever NNTP server you want and retrieves net news articles, while displaying exactly what is sent from the server. The user can also send line by line back to the remote.

title: Our Family Tree 1.2
description: Shareware $10.00 An easy way to make a family tree. From pedigrees to desendents, it's an easy way to make a real family tree. This program also recomends use of a font called 'Goudy Medieval'

title: Film Script
description: This stack is for all those budding screenwriters with a Mac. I developed this stack to get all those itty-bitty thoughts onto paper, you know, the ones that just don't fit anywhere? Anyway, Film script has several cards that should be pretty self-explanatory, but do try all buttons for added tips. Also, I don't recommend using command-recent to leave the stack. You'll get an annoying little script problem. Just ignore it. I plan to fix that in the next version. I also plan to make the 'note buttons'moveable so that you can organize your 'notes' in the order you like. Film Script is shareware--if you use it, it'll cost you just $20 to get the newer version, plus a discount toward TV Scripts. Yes, I'm also working on a TV script version which differs greatly from the Film script version as far as format goes. I'm always interested in your comments and suggestions. Please e-mail me: orbie.

title: Student Journal.sea
description: expand the journal as much as you need. It has a friendly interface, and is easy to expand. Use with as many students as you want by registering your master copy. ------ Dancing Rabbit Creations produces educational HyperCard programs and games for teachers, students, parents, and learners of all ages. The stacks are affordably priced, and users can try them before they buy - all are released as shareware, or are available in demonstration versions. Dancing Rabbit Creations is the joint (ad)venture of a classroom teacher and a software tester.

title: PopAlert XFCN
description: ----------------- Create custom alerts for your stacks with this external function.

title: +Living with HIV Disease
description: This Hypercard Stack has been created by a group of Graduate Nursing Students at the University of Massachusetts at Boston. We all have been involved in healthcare for many years. We all share a strong interest in facilitating and improving the quality of life of people living with chronic HIV disease and in providing the best form of resource advocacy that we can. People coping with HIV disease have very unique and special needs, and these needs are the focus of this stack. We've created this stack as a seed to be planted in Cyberspace. We hope that you will add resources which you think might be of value. Please continue to pass the stack along. Please add your name to our list, provide some information about yourself, if you choose, and include your Email address if you'd be willing to act as a resource person, or simply if you'd like to keep in touch. We ask that you leave the name of the stack unchanged, this will enable us to locate the stack in the future. Thanks for your help. Be happy.... (Staff note: Good info, well done. Simple interface, but effective and moving.)

title: Speech Utilities demo
description: Allows you to access virtually all of the speech manager routines from within HyperCard via both XCMDs and XFCNs.

title: AIATSIS word lists
description: Words from Australian Indigenous languages

title: McPoet 2.0
description: 'Does for poetry what McDonalds did for food.' After entering your variables, write hysterically pretensious poetry at the click of a button.

title: Media Center
description: ----------------- This is a HyperCard 1.2.5 stack that will help you organize and manage your video collection.

title: Jurassic Park Security
description: ace shown in the book Jurassic Park. Not like the movie, but looks pretty technical and is as confusing as the book says it was. Send me mail about it! -DavidT1953

title: Klingon Dictionary
description: This stack is programmed in HyperCard. I thought about it when I went to the shops to get this fantastic book named "The Klingon Dictionary". This was paper. I wanted to have a dicitionary for Klingon in a electronic version. This stack took about two weeks of work. Most of this time was filled with hacking the words in the files and programming a Stack to translate the phonetic letters into Klingon letters. But it's done and it's well done.

title: MacChef 2.0.3
description: I first wrote MacChef in 1995, after becoming frustrated with the limitations of a recipe program I had downloaded a few weeks earlier. Oh, the program worked all right, but it worked the way the writer wanted, not the way I wanted. If I rarely bake bread or pastry, why do I need a Bread & Pastry category of recipes? On the other hand, if I make a lot of pasta, why can't I add a Pasta category? MacChef allows me (and you) to do that kind of thing. I also wanted to make importing text files as easy as possible with a minimum of editing and reformatting. That gives MacChef a plain interface, but it saves time and effort when downloading recipes from the Web and the rec.food.recipes newsgroup, the source of many recipes in this collection. In the four years since I wrote the original MacChef, the number of recipes has grown to more than 500. I haven't tried all of them, but they all looked very promising when I downloaded them from rec.food.recipes and other sources. In each case, I've given credit to the original developer of the recipe if I knew who that person was. Many of the recipes include comments by the person who wrote the recipe. My own comments are attributed to "Bob." And you'll see several recipes I've developed myself. As a cardiac patient, I usually try to come up with recipes that are low in fat and sodium while still tasting great. (See my Low Fat Manicotti for example.)

title: Sez's Sound Sampler #1.Sit

title: The Bee
description: A bee randomly flies around in a room with a personâs face in it. If the bee lands on the personâs nose it will sting them and their eyes will roll around (like little marbles). If the bee hits any solid wall 10 times it will turn upside down and gently glide to the bottom, dead. If the bee flies through one of the four openings in each room, the little window will animate so that it looks like the room is shifting to the side to display another room with another, different person. This room movement will go on forever with the bee buzzing and bumping into walls (making a bump noise) until the bee dies or the user gets tired of this stupid thing and holds down a key to stop it. Iâm sorry, I canât remember the key at the moment but experiment and comment when you fine it. Note: there are a few easer eggs in The Bee: certain faces have âwormholesâ through their ears and if the bee enters one of them it will disappear, seemingly banging around inside their head, then emerge, either out of their other ear, or, in a special case, the window will shift and the bee will come out another personâs ear in another room. Enjoy. For more on the history of The Bee: https://richardsnotes.org/2005/03/24/the-bee/

This stack provides the album title, composer(s), label, and miscellaneous notes (contents, reissue info, availability, country of origin, existence of liner notes, etc.) and can be sorted alphabetically by several categories. It also features three checkboxes in which to check off your own particular attitude toward the album ('Got it,' 'Want it,' or 'Skip it'), and you can then sort by this criteria as well. If you're interested in film scores, you'll find this stack useful and fun. If not, you probably won't.

title: Caper in the Castro
description: Artist Statement, 2023: "I regret the spelling errors found in the game. I am mildly dyslexic and suck at spelling. Back in 1988, I had no one to edit my writing. I also regret some of the more "cringe-worthy" humor that is inappropriate by today's standards. In some older video reviews as well as interviews, people have used the pronouns "she and her" when describing me. I am transgender and my preferred pronouns are "They, Them, and Their." Note: By default, the sound is off on this emulation. When the desktop boots up, click on the Apple icon in the upper left, select CONTROL PANEL, then click on SOUND and bring the volume level from 0 to 9. This will enable sound during the game. Programmed by CM Ralph (https://cmralph.com/) in 1989, Caper in the Castro is likely the first LGBT game. It was distributed via BBS, and is 'Charityware', with the designer asking players to donate to the AIDS Charity of their choice. You are the world famous lesbian private detective, Tracker McDyke. You are searching for a kidnapped drag queen, Tessy LaFemme. What you didn't count on was stumbling onto an even larger and more treacherous crime. The game was changed to different locations and a heterosexual them released under the name "Murder on Main Street". This version is located here . For more information about "Caper in the Castro": https://www.npr.org/2023/01/27/1151702216/how-the-first-lgbtq-video-game-was-given-a-second-life A Q&A With The Designer of the First LGBT Computer Game , by Luke Winkie, Paste Magazine, June 9, 2014. Caper in the Castro in the LGBTQ Video Game Archive. --------The following is taken from the original description / info text file:-------------- About "Caper in the Castro" This game is a Gay and Lesbian based adventure mystery featuring graphics, text, animation and sound. Written with HyperCard® Version 1.2. (Earlier versions will not be compatible) It is the first Gay and Lesbian computer game to be published in the world, as far as we know. In the game you assume the role of a lesbian private detective, Tracker McDyke, in search of a kidnapped drag queen, Tessy LaFemme. What you stumble onto is an even bigger and more hideous crime with a mystery that surrounds it. (Who is Dullagan Straightman? Who is the mysterious "L.L." that Straightman works for? What is the meaning of the cryptograph?) The game has a built-in help/tutorial if you get stuck, the best advice is to try everything and anything during your adventure. You never know what might happen! The game is packed with inside humor, puns and lots of perplexing problems. About the Author C.M. Ralph is a free lance artist living in the San Francisco Bay area. She supports her indulgence in art and computer game developing by working as a Quality Control Technician. Her work as an artist has spanned some 20 years and includes such notables as the design of the AIDS Response Program logo for Southern California's Orange County chapter, along with much donated work for many other nonprofit Gay and Lesbian Organizations. This game, as she describes it is "CharityWare" follows in this tradition of works for the Gay and Lesbian community. It is given out freely, with her request that if you enjoy playing it, please make a donation to an AIDS Related charity of your choice for whatever amount you feel is appropriate. The Inside Poop Caper in the Castro had a creation time of 6 months, It began in HyperCard version 1.0 and then was upgraded during construction. There was an additional 3 months of test playing in it's beta version. The final game version 1.0 was released in May of1989. Thank You's and credits appear when you click on the "About Game" button at the opening of the game. However there are some add-on's to be mentioned here: Thanks go to Diane, the sysop of Alternatives BBS (415) 922-5489 , for putting me in touch with Bill, the sysop of Fog City BBS (415) 863-9697 , (Who was enthusiastic enough to carry this game, thus allowing it nation-wide distribution.) Thank you Bill.

title: CompileIt!
description: CompileIt! is the first true compiler for HyperTalk, the programming language for non-programmers on the Apple Macintosh. Before CompileIt!, HyperTalk scripts were constrained to run slowly in HyperCard - with no protection from prying eyes! The only alternative was to abandon the ease and comfort of HyperTalk for a conventional language such as C or Pascal. CompileIt! makes available the power and speed of these languages, without requiring that you learn a new language and new programming environment. CompileIt! translates the HyperTalk language into 68000 machine language, and packages the result as a HyperCard external command (XCMD) or function (XFCN) which can then be used from any HyperCard script. CompileIt! also provides many useful extensions to the HyperTalk language which will give you access to features of the Macintosh far beyond what HyperCard alone provides. Michel, Steve. (1994). CompileIt! User Manual . Heizer Software. Pleasant Hill, CA. Compilelt! 2.0 is a significant upgrade to Heizer Software's HyperTalk compiler. This version supports HyperCard 2.0's new XCMD interface and is significantly faster than the previous version. Compilelt! allows anyone familiar with HyperCard's scripting language to write external commands and functions that extend HyperCards capabilities. A Stack-Based Compiler Since HyperCard was introduced in 1987, its XCMD interface has been its most powerful feature. By writing code that conforms to the XCMD-interface format and installing it in a stack's resource fork, programmers can add commands and functions that customize HyperCard’s scripting language. CompileIt!’s mandate is to give the power of customization to those unfamiliar with conventional programming languages such as Pascal or C. Compilelt! is unique in that it is implemented as a HyperCard stack and its programming language is HyperTalk. External commands and functions were given those monikers because Apple programmers expected them to be developed outside of HyperCard, in another programing 1anguage. CompileIt!, however, makes developing externals possible within HyperCard. Although it's implemented as a HyperCard stack, Compilelt! isn’t limited to HyperCard. XCMDs developed with Compilelt! can be installed and used in SuperCard projects, for example, or with other products, such as Authorware and MacroMind Director, that support XCMDs. To create XCMDs with Compilelt!, you type or paste HyperTalk scripts into fields in the Compilelt! stack and click on a button. Compilelt! subsequently compiles the code, installs it in the resource fork of the target slack, and sends you the stack so that you can test the XCMD. CompileIt! provides various indications of its progress during compilation, which can run in the background under MultiFinder. Here's an indication of its compilation speed: A 40-line test script took only about three minutes to compile into a 2,400-byte XFCN when running in the background on an 8-megabyte Macintosh II. You'll probably need to revisit the HyperTalk code to debug or improve it. CompileIt!, which has all the basic components of a programming environment, can assist you with all the tools you need to edit and debug code as well as analyze code performance, Getting the Bugs Out One of these tools, DebugIt!, is new in version 2.0. DebugIt! is a source-level debugger you can install in the external (adding 40K to the external’s size). With DebugIt! installed, you can see (but not modify) the source code, set break points, single-step through the code, and examine and edit the values of variables. You can also halt execution, send HyperCard an arbitrary HyperTalk message, and resume execution. According to Heizer, DebugIt! is not dependent on any HyperCard-specific features beyond those in HyperCard 1.0. so it should work with any application that supports externals (Heizer has tested it with all the versions of HyperCard and SuperCard), CompileIt! partially executes its translation by looking up symbols in a symbol table. The symbol table contains not only the names of HyperTalk functions but also the names of those unique to SuperTalk and the names of more than 500 Mac ROM Toolbox routines. The latter are gateways to real Mac programming, CompileIt!’s manual gives a good but brief introduction to ROM calls, which are covered in more depth in Inside Macintosh. Some programming experience is required to use ROM calls. Not only do they speed up code but the roughly 100 Color QuickDraw routines are also useful for SuperCard developers or for anyone developing external windows. Beginners may stumble when it comes to using numerics and string variables from within CompileIt!, which isn't as forgiving as HyperTalk in the use of these data types. Novices also need to be aware that even if a HyperTalk script compiles perfectly in CompileIt!, it may tend to behave in subtly different ways. This happens because the external cannot occupy exactly the same place in the HyperCard message hierarchy as a HyperTalk script does. Also, because an external, unlike a script, is not associated with an object, the HyperTalk term me, which refers to the script’s object, doesn’t make sense in an external and is not supported. Although CompileIt!'s manual represents a solid effort (users with some HyperTalk experience will be able to grasp the fundamentals), only programmers with broader experience will he able to make complete sense of it. Even though it acknowledges that it's possible to use CompileIt! to develop other kinds of software, such as Fkeys, the manual doesn't cover such advanced use. The on-line documentation covers most of the manual topics in less depth, and there’s Balloon Help for System 7 users. In addition to the compiler and the 200-page manual, the CompileIt! package contains a demo stack, a batch-compiling stack, Steve Mailer’s ResCopy, a folder of alternative symbol tables, and HyperCard. The program requires 750K of memory, but Heizer recommends 2 megabytes. To its credit, CompileIt! is clever with memory. As far back as 1975, when 4K was a lot of memory, Tom Pittman, CompileIt!'s creator, wrote a complete BASIC interpreter and sold it for $5 per copy. He understands how to write memory-efficient code. The Bottom Line Although the original version of CompileIt! was so slow, that it wasn’t practical for serious development work, version 2.0 features much-improved compilation speed and supports the latest version of HyperCard, With its unique implementation as a HyperCard stack, CompileIt! makes the process of creating XCMDs and XFCNs accessible to anyone who's familiar with HyperTalk. Swaine, Michael. (February 1992). CompileIt! 2.0. MacUser . (pgs. 74, 76).

title: Uncle Buddy's Phantom Funhouse
description: Art "Buddy" Newkirk has disappeared and left you his literary estate. By the looks of it, he and his friends were a very odd bunch. You might have enjoyed knowing them. But you don't: why does "Uncle" Buddy think you do? Where is he, anyway? And what does this have to do with Meister Eckhart and the New York City subway? To find out, you'll have to pop the floppies into your Mac, drop the tapes into your boombox, and get ready to meet Buddy's friends, read his email, listen to his band, and sort out his (very strange) Tarot deck. Surreal and humorous, this is a world you will often return to and long remember.
